event_description: Noah was FIVE HUNDRED YEARS OLD, and Noah became the father of Shem, Ham, and Japheth. (GEN 5:32)
event_year_calculation: The year of Shem_1's birth is the year his father, Noah_1, was born (1057) + the age of his father at Shem_1's birth (503) = 1560
event_notes:
The Flood ENDED when Noah was 601 (GEN 8:13). 
Two years after the Flood ENDED, Noah was 603 years old. (math)
Two years after the Flood ENDED, Shem is 100 years old and sires Arpachshad (GEN 11:10).
This means that Shem was born 100 years prior, in the 503rd year of Noah's life.

Unless they were triplets (unlikely) and all born when Noah was 500 years old (GEN 5:32), Noah's sons were born in different years AFTER he reached the age of 500.  Genesis 9:22, 24 state that Ham is the youngest.  However, neither the Hebrew and Greek texts of Genesis 10:21 are clear as to whether Shem or Japheth is the elder son.
Ussher has Japheth as the elder son born when Noah is 500 years old in 1556 AM/2448 BCE

[Shulman date p15]
[Ussher date p19]

event_description: In the SIX HUNDREDTH YEAR of Noah's life, in the second month, on the seventeenth day of the month, on the same day all the fountains of the great deep burst open, and the floodgates of the sky were opened. The rain fell upon the earth for forty days and forty nights. (GEN 7:11-12)
event_year_calculation: The year the Flood began is the year of Noah_1's birth (1057) + his age when the Flood began (600) = 1657
event_notes:
[Shulman date p15]

event_description_id: Dalam ENAM RATUS TAHUN kehidupan Nuh, pada bulan kedua, pada hari ketujuh belas bulan itu, pada hari yang sama semua mata air samudera raya terbuka, dan pintu-pintu air di langit terbuka. Hujan turun ke bumi selama empat puluh hari empat puluh malam. (Kejadian 7:11-12)

event_description: Now it came about in the SIX HUNDRED AND FIRST YEAR, in the first month, on the first of the month, the water was dried up from the earth. Then Noah removed the covering of the ark, and looked, and behold, the surface of the ground was dried up. (GEN 8:13)
event_year_calculation: The year the Flood ended is the year of Noah_1's birth (1057) + his age when the Flood ended (601) = 1658
event_notes:
The Flood began on the 17th day of the 2nd month during Noah's 600th year of life (GEN 7:11).
Noah's birth date could have fallen on ANY date after that up to the 1st day of the 1st month the following year since the Flood ended in the 601st year of Noah's life (GEN 8:13).  The water abated and the land was dry at the end of the second month (GEN 8:14).
[Shulman date p15]
[Ussher date p19]
event_description_id: Sekarang terjadilah pada tahun ENAM RATUS PERTAMA, pada bulan pertama, pada tanggal satu bulan itu, air telah mengering dari dalam bumi. Kemudian Nuh membuka penutup bahtera itu, dan tampaklah, permukaan tanah menjadi kering. (Kejadian 8:13)

event_name: G-d makes a covenant with Noah
event_description: Then God spoke to Noah and to his sons with him, saying, "Now behold, I Myself do establish My covenant with you, and with your descendants after you; and with every living creature that is with you, the birds, the cattle, and every beast of the earth with you; of all that comes out of the ark, even every beast of the earth.  I establish My covenant with you; and all flesh shall never again be cut off by the water of the flood, neither shall there again be a flood to destroy the earth." (GEN 9:8-11)
event_year_calculation: The year of Noah's covenant is the year the Flood ended = 1658
event_notes:
[Shulman date p15]
[Ussher date p21]
event_description_id: Kemudian Allah berfirman kepada Nuh dan anak-anaknya yang bersamanya, bersabda, “Sesungguhnya, Aku sendiri yang mengadakan perjanjian-Ku denganmu, dan dengan keturunanmu setelah kamu; dan dengan segala makhluk hidup yang bersamamu, burung-burung, ternak, dan segala binatang di bumi yang bersamamu; dari semua yang keluar dari bahtera, bahkan segala binatang di bumi. Aku mengadakan perjanjian-Ku denganmu; dan segala makhluk tidak akan pernah lagi dilenyapkan oleh air bah, dan tidak akan ada lagi air bah yang membinasakan bumi.” (Kejadian 9:8-11)

event_description:
Terah lived SEVENTY YEARS, and became the father of Abram, Nahor and Haran. (GEN 11:26)
The days of Terah were TWO HUNDRED AND FIVE YEARS; and Terah died in Haran. (GEN 11:32)
Now Abram was SEVENTY-FIVE YEARS old when he departed from Haran. (GEN 12:4)
[Terah was 205 when he died in Haran and Abram was 75 left Haran when Terah died (Acts 7:4).
This means Terah was 130 when Abram was born so either Nahor or Haran was born when Terah was 70.]
event_year_calculation: The year of Haran_1's birth is the year his father, Terah_1, was born (1880) + the age of his father at Haran_1's birth (70) = 1950
event_notes:
*HYPOTHESIS:
Terah was at least 70 years old when Abram was born. (GEN 11:26)
Unless they were triplets, Abram, Nahor, and Haran were not born the same year.
Terah was 205 when he died in Haran. Abram was 75 and left Haran when Terah died (Acts 7:4).
This means Terah was 130 (math: 205-75) when Abram was born and either Nahor or Haran was born to Terah when he was 70.
No other details are available for Nahor so my hypothesis is that Haran was the one born when Terah was 70.
[Ussher date p22]
event_description_id:
Terah hidup TUJUH PULUH TAHUN, dan menjadi ayah Abram, Nahor dan Haran. (Kejadian 11:26)
Umur Terah adalah DUA RATUS LIMA TAHUN; dan Terah meninggal di Haran. (Kejadian 11:32)
Abram berumur TUJUH PULUH LIMA TAHUN ketika ia berangkat dari Haran. (Kejadian 12:4)
[Terah berusia 205 tahun ketika dia meninggal di Haran dan Abram berusia 75 tahun meninggalkan Haran ketika Terah meninggal (Kisah Para Rasul 7:4).
Ini berarti Terah berusia 130 tahun ketika Abram lahir, jadi Nahor atau Haran lahir ketika Terah berusia 70 tahun.]

event_year_calculation: The year of Peleg_1's death is the year his son, Reu_1, was born  (1789) + the years Peleg_1 lived after his son's birth (209) = 1998
event_notes:
30 years + 209 years = 239 years total.
The FIRST death after the Flood recorded in Scripture was Peleg, a man born AFTER the Flood.
[Shulman date p18]

event_name: Tower of Babel and the dispersion of humanity*
event_description:
They said, “Come, let us build for ourselves a city, and a tower whose top will reach into heaven, and let us make for ourselves a name, otherwise we will be scattered abroad over the face of the whole earth.”
“Come, let Us go down and there confuse their language, so that they will not understand one another’s speech.”
So the LORD scattered them abroad from there over the face of the whole earth; and they stopped building the city. (GEN 11:4, 7-8)
event_year_calculation: The year of the Tower of Babel and the dispersion of humanity is the year of Peleg's death = 1998
event_notes:
*HYPOTHESIS:
Gen 10:25 notes Peleg [Hebrew: פָּלַג - to divide] was named such "for in his days the earth was divided".  The Tower of Babel and the dispersion of humanity must have occurred before his death.
EXTRA-BIBLICAL SOURCE:
Seder Olam Rabbah states that the dispersion was 340 years after the Flood.
[Shulman date p21]
Ussher states: Nineteen hundred and three years elapsed from this time to the capture of Babylon by Alexander the Great.  This calculation was made according to the astronomical observations by Porphyry and the number of years is as we find recorded in Simplicius {Simplicius, De Caelo, 1.2}
[Ussher date p22]
peristiwa: Menara Babel dan penyebaran umat manusia*
event_description_id:
Kata mereka: “Marilah kita dirikan sendiri sebuah kota dan sebuah menara yang puncaknya sampai ke langit, dan marilah kita cari nama, kalau tidak kita akan tercerai-berai ke seluruh bumi.”
“Marilah Kami turun dan mengacaubalaukan bahasa mereka di sana, sehingga mereka tidak memahami pembicaraan satu sama lain.”
Maka TUHAN menyebarkan mereka dari sana ke seluruh muka bumi; dan mereka berhenti membangun kota itu. (Kejadian 11:4, 7-8)

Now the promises were spoken to Abraham and to his seed. He does not say, "And to seeds," as referring to many, but rather to one, "And to your seed," that is, Christ.  What I am saying is this: the Law, which came FOUR HUNDRED AND THIRTY YEARS later, does not invalidate a covenant previously ratified by God, so as to nullify the promise. (GAL 3:16-17)
event_year_calculation: The year Abram receives the Promise is the year of the Covenant at Sinai (2515) - the years between the Covenant and the Promise (430) = 2085
event_notes:

event_notes:
34 years + 430 years = 464 years total

event_name: G-d makes a covenant with Isaac*
event_description: The LORD appeared to him and said, "Do not go down to Egypt; stay in the land of which I shall tell you.  Sojourn in this land and I will be with you and bless you, for to you and to your descendants I will give all these lands, and I will establish the oath which I swore to your father Abraham. (GEN 26:2-3)
event_year_calculation: The year of Isaac's covenant is the year of Abram_1's death (2185) + 10 years (estimated) = 2195
event_notes:
*HYPOTHESIS: 
Isaac dug the wells that had been stopped up (GEN 26:18) AFTER Abraham died.
Redigging the wells happened AFTER Isaac lived in Gerar and offended Abimelech (GEN 26:10) then moved into the valley (GEN 26:17).
The covenant conversation (GEN 26:2-3) happens BEFORE Isaac went to Gerar (GEN 26;6).
Allowing for 10 years after Abraham's death for the wells to be stopped up.
[Ussher date p28]
event_description_id: TUHAN menampakkan diri kepadanya dan berkata, "Jangan pergi ke Mesir; tinggallah di negeri yang akan Kuberitahukan kepadamu. Tinggallah di negeri ini dan Aku akan menyertaimu dan memberkatimu, sebab seluruh negeri ini akan Kuberikan kepadamu dan kepada keturunanmu, dan Aku akan menepati sumpah yang telah Kujanjikan kepada ayahmu Abraham. (GEN 26:2-3)

event_name: Jacob deceives Isaac for the blessing
event_description: And he said, "Your brother came deceitfully and has taken away your blessing." Then he said, "Is he not rightly named Jacob, for he has supplanted me these two times? He took away my birthright, and behold, now he has taken away my blessing." And he said, "Have you not reserved a blessing for me?" (GEN 27:35-36)
event_year_calculation: The year of Jacob's deception is the same year he departed to Paddan-aram = 2247
event_notes:
[Shulman date p23]
[Ussher date p29]
peristiwa: Yakub menipu Ishak untuk mendapatkan berkat
event_description_id: Dan dia berkata, “Adikmu datang dengan penuh tipu daya dan telah merampas berkahmu.” Katanya lagi: "Tidak pantaskah ia menamai Yakub, karena ia telah menggantikan aku dua kali ini? Ia merampas hak kesulunganku, dan lihatlah, sekarang ia telah merampas berkatku." Dan dia berkata, "Apakah kamu tidak menyediakan berkah untukku?" (Kejadian 27:35-36)

event_description: So Isaac called Jacob and blessed him and charged him, and said to him, "You shall not take a wife from the daughters of Canaan.  Arise, go to Paddan-aram, to the house of Bethuel your mother’s father; and from there take to yourself a wife from the daughters of Laban your mother’s brother. (GEN 28:1-2)
event_year_calculation: The year Jacob_1 departed to Paddan-aram is the year his son, Joseph_1, was born (2261) - the years he served Laban before his son's birth (14) = 2247
event_notes:
Now Joseph was THIRTY YEARS old when he stood before Pharaoh, king of Egypt. And Joseph went out from the presence of Pharaoh and went through all the land of Egypt. (GEN 41:46) [Joseph is made viceroy at age 30 and would have been 37 at the end of the seven years of plenty. (math)] 

For the famine has been in the land these TWO YEARS, and there are still five years in which there will be neither plowing nor harvesting. (GEN 45:6) 
[Jacob goes to Egypt in the second of the seven years of famine when Joseph would have been 39 (math).]

So Jacob said to Pharaoh, "The years of my sojourning are ONE HUNDRED AND THIRTY; few and unpleasant have been the years of my life, nor have they attained the years that my fathers lived during the days of their sojourning." (GEN 47:9) 
[Jacob was 130 when he entered the land and Joseph was 39. Jacob would have been 91 when Joseph was born. (math)]

Jacob had served Laban for 14 years when Joseph was born (Jacob told Laban he wanted to return to his country- GEN 30:25) and must have completed his 7 years of service for Leah and 7 years of service for Rachel.

Jacob was 91 years old when Joseph was born.  Subtracting Jacob's 14 years of service to Laban means Jacob was 77 years old when he left for Padan Aram.
[Shulman date p23]
[Ussher date p29]
event_description_id: Maka Ishak memanggil Yakub dan memberkatinya serta menuntut dia, lalu berkata kepadanya, "Jangan mengambil isteri dari anak-anak perempuan Kanaan. Bangunlah, pergilah ke Padan-aram, ke rumah Betuel ayah ibumu; dan dari sana ambillah bagimu isteri dari anak-anak perempuan Laban, saudara ibumu. (GEN 28:1-2)

event_description: So Esau saw that the daughters of Canaan displeased his father Isaac; and Esau went to Ishmael, and married, besides the wives that he had, Mahalath the daughter of Ishmael, Abraham’s son, the sister of Nebaioth. (GEN 28:8-9)

event_description: So Jacob served SEVEN YEARS for Rachel and they seemed to him but a few days because of his love for her. (GEN 29:20)
event_year_calculation: The year Jacob_1 married Leah_1 is the year he departed for Paddan-aram (2247) + the years he served Laban for Rachel (7) = 2254.  He married Rachel_1 a week later. (GEN 29:27)
event_notes:
Jacob served Laban for 7 years before his marriage to Leah (GEN 29:23).  
A week later (GEN 29:27-28) he married Rachel and then served Laban another 7 years after their marriage.

Zilpa, pembantu Lea, melahirkan seorang anak laki-laki bagi Yakub. Lalu Leah berkata, "Betapa beruntungnya!" Jadi dia menamainya Gad. (Kejadian 30:9-11)

Kemudian Lea berkata, "Tuhan telah menganugerahiku dengan pemberian yang baik; sekarang suamiku akan tinggal bersamaku, karena aku telah memberinya enam orang anak laki-laki." Jadi dia menamainya Zebulon. (Kejadian 30:19-20)

event_description: Afterward she bore a daughter and named her Dinah. (GEN 30:21)
event_year_calculation: The year of Dinah_1's birth is the year Jacob_1 married Leah_1 (2254) + time for her to conceive and bear daughter (7) = 2261 (thought to be a twin of Zebulun).
event_notes:
*HYPOTHESIS: 
Jacob's marriage to Leah + ~7 years to conceive and bear another child.  

*EXTRA-BIBLICAL SOURCE:
Eliezer Shulman's "The Sequence of Events in the Old Testament" (page 28) places Dinah's birth in the year 2199 AM as a twin of Zebulun, 7 years after Leah is given to Jacob in marriage.  "Afterward" in GEN 30:21 is taken to mean MINUTES afterward.

*EXTRA-BIBLICAL HISTORY:
Dinah is thought to be the twin of Zebulun.
See Ibn Ezra on Genesis 30:21 and Talmud, Bava Batra 123a.

event_name: Jacob wrestles with the angel and has his name changed to Israel
event_description: Then Jacob was left alone, and a man wrestled with him until daybreak.  When he saw that he had not prevailed against him, he touched the socket of his thigh; so the socket of Jacob’s thigh was dislocated while he wrestled with him.  Then he said, "Let me go, for the dawn is breaking." But he said, "I will not let you go unless you bless me."  (GEN 32:24-26)
event_year_calculation: The year Jacob_1 wrestles with the angel is the year he departed Paddan-aram to return to Canaan (2267) = 2267
event_notes:
[Shulman date p35]
[Ussher date p30]
peristiwa: Yakub bergumul dengan malaikat dan namanya diubah menjadi Israel
event_description_id: Lalu tinggallah Yakub seorang diri dan ada seorang laki-laki yang bergumul dengannya sampai fajar menyingsing.  Ketika dia melihat bahwa dia tidak dapat mengalahkannya, dia menyentuh pangkal pahanya; jadi pangkal paha Yakub terkilir saat dia bergulat dengannya.  Lalu dia berkata, "Biarkan aku pergi, karena fajar sudah menyingsing." Namun dia berkata, "Aku tidak akan membiarkanmu pergi kecuali kamu memberkatiku."  (Kejadian 32:24-26)

event_description: Now Deborah, Rebekah’s nurse, died, and she was buried below Bethel under the oak; it was named Allon-bacuth. (GEN 35:8)

event_description: It came about as her soul was departing (for she died), that she named him Ben-oni; but his father called him Benjamin. So Rachel died and was buried on the way to Ephrath (that is, Bethlehem). (GEN 35:18-19)

event_notes:

event_description_id: Ketika jiwanya pergi (karena dia meninggal), dia menamainya Ben-oni; tapi ayahnya memanggilnya Benyamin. Maka Rahel meninggal dan dikuburkan dalam perjalanan ke Efrat (yaitu Betlehem). (Kejadian 35:18-19)

event_description: These are the records of the generations of Jacob. Joseph, when SEVENTEEN YEARS of age, was pasturing the flock with his brothers while he was still a youth, along with the sons of Bilhah and the sons of Zilpah, his father's wives. And Joseph brought back a bad report about them to their father. Now Israel loved Joseph more than all his sons, because he was the son of his old age; and he made him a varicolored tunic. His brothers saw that their father loved him more than all his brothers; and so they hated him and could not speak to him on friendly terms. Then Joseph had a dream, and when he told it to his brothers, they hated him even more. (GEN 37:2-5)
event_year_calculation: The year of Joseph_1's vision is the year he was born (2261) + his age when he had the vision (17) = 2278
event_notes:
[Shulman date p33]
[Ussher date p30]
event_description_id: Inilah catatan generasi Yakub. Ketika Yusuf berumur TUJUH BELAS TAHUN, ketika ia masih muda, ia sedang menggembalakan kawanan domba bersama saudara-saudaranya, bersama dengan anak-anak Bilha dan anak-anak Zilpa, istri-istri bapaknya. Dan Yusuf membawa kembali laporan buruk tentang mereka kepada ayah mereka. Kini Israel lebih menyayangi Yusuf daripada semua putranya, karena ia adalah putra di masa tuanya; dan dia membuatkannya tunik beraneka warna. Saudara laki-lakinya melihat bahwa ayah mereka lebih menyayanginya daripada semua saudara laki-lakinya; maka mereka membencinya dan tidak dapat berbicara ramah kepadanya. Lalu Yusuf bermimpi, dan ketika ia menceritakan hal itu kepada saudara-saudaranya, mereka semakin membencinya. (Kejadian 37:2-5)

event_description: Now the days of Isaac were ONE HUNDRED AND EIGHTY YEARS. (GEN 35:28)

event_description_id: Kini umur Ishak adalah SERIUS DELAPAN PULUH TAHUN. (Kejadian 35:28)

event_description: So Pharaoh said to Joseph, "Since God has informed you of all this, there is no one so discerning and wise as you are. You shall be over my house, and according to your command all my people shall do homage; only in the throne I will be greater than you." Pharaoh said to Joseph, "See, I have set you over all the land of Egypt." Then Pharaoh took off his signet ring from his hand and put it on Joseph's hand, and clothed him in garments of fine linen and put the gold necklace around his neck. He had him ride in his second chariot; and they proclaimed before him, "Bow the knee!" And he set him over all the land of Egypt. Moreover, Pharaoh said to Joseph, "Though I am Pharaoh, yet without your permission no one shall raise his hand or foot in all the land of Egypt." Then Pharaoh named Joseph Zaphenath-paneah; and he gave him Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera priest of On, as his wife. And Joseph went forth over the land of Egypt. Now Joseph was THIRTY YEARS OLD when he stood before Pharaoh, king of Egypt. And Joseph went out from the presence of Pharaoh and went through all the land of Egypt. (GEN 41:39-46)
event_year_calculation: The year Joseph_1's time as viceroy began is the year he was born (2261) + his age when he became viceroy (30) = 2291
event_notes:
[Shulman date p33]
[Ussher date p30]
event_description_id: Maka Firaun berkata kepada Yusuf, "Karena Allah telah memberitahukan kepadamu tentang semua ini, tidak ada seorang pun yang begitu cerdas dan bijaksana seperti kamu. Kamu harus menjadi kepala rumahku, dan sesuai perintahmu seluruh umatku harus memberi hormat; hanya di takhta aku akan lebih besar dari kamu." Firaun berkata kepada Yusuf, “Lihatlah, aku telah mengangkat engkau menjadi penguasa seluruh tanah Mesir.” Kemudian Firaun melepaskan cincin meterai dari tangannya dan memasangkannya pada tangan Yusuf, lalu mengenakannya dengan pakaian dari linen halus dan mengalungkan kalung emas pada lehernya. Dia menyuruhnya menaiki kereta keduanya; dan mereka berseru di hadapannya, "Berlutut!" Dan dia mengangkatnya menjadi penguasa seluruh tanah Mesir. Terlebih lagi, Firaun berkata kepada Yusuf, “Meskipun aku Firaun, namun tanpa izinmu tidak ada seorang pun yang boleh mengangkat tangan atau kakinya di seluruh tanah Mesir.” Kemudian Firaun menamai Yusuf Zafenat-paneah; dan dia memberinya Asenath, putri pendeta Potifera di On, sebagai istrinya. Dan Yusuf berangkat melintasi tanah Mesir. Sekarang Yusuf berumur TIGA PULUH TAHUN ketika dia berdiri di hadapan Firaun, raja Mesir. Dan Yusuf keluar dari hadapan Firaun dan menjelajahi seluruh tanah Mesir. (Kejadian 41:39-46)

event_description: Now these are the names of the sons of Israel, Jacob and his sons, who went to Egypt: Reuben, Jacob's firstborn... The sons of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari. (GEN 46:8, 11)
event_year_calculation: The year of Kohath_1's birth is the year his father, Levi_1 was born (2257) + the age of his father at Kohath_1's birth (43) = 2300
event_notes:
*EXTRA-BIBLICAL SOURCE:
Kohath was already born when the Israelites went down to Egypt.
Eliezer Shulman's "The Sequence of Events in the Old Testament" (page 41) places Kohath's birth in the year 2238AM, when Levi is 43 years old.
event_description_id: Inilah nama anak-anak Israel, Yakub dan anak-anaknya, yang pergi ke Mesir: Ruben, anak sulung Yakub... Anak-anak Lewi: Gerson, Kohat, dan Merari. (Kejadian 46:8, 11)

event_description:
They took their livestock and their property, which they had acquired in the land of Canaan, and came to Egypt, Jacob and all his descendants with him: (GEN 46:6)
So Jacob said to Pharaoh, "The years of my sojourning are ONE HUNDRED AND THIRTY; few and unpleasant have been the years of my life, nor have they attained the years that my fathers lived during the days of their sojourning." (GEN 47:9)
event_year_calculation: The year Jacob_1 went to Egypt is his birth year (2170) + his age when he arrives and speaks to Pharaoh (130) = 2300
event_notes:
Jacob was 130 years old when he arrived in Egypt and spoke with Pharaoh (GEN 47:9) in the second year of the famine (GEN 45:6-9)

Jacob lived to be 147 years old and had arrived in Egypt 17 years earlier (GEN 47:28) .  

Both GEN 47:9 (directly) and GEN 47:28 (via math 147-17=130) affirm that Jacob arrived in Egypt when he was 130 years old. 
** THIS DATE IS CRITICAL TO CALCULATING JOSEPH'S BIRTH. **
[Shulman date p41]
[Ussher date p31]
event_description_id:
Mereka membawa ternak dan harta benda mereka, yang diperoleh mereka di tanah Kanaan, lalu datang ke Mesir, bersama Yakub dan seluruh keturunannya: (GEN 46:6)
Maka berkatalah Yakub kepada Firaun, "Tahun-tahun persinggahanku adalah SATU RATUS TIGA PULUH; tahun-tahun hidupku sedikit dan tidak menyenangkan, dan tahun-tahun itu tidak mencapai tahun-tahun yang ditinggali nenek moyangku selama mereka tinggal di sana." (Kejadian 47:9)

event_description: The birth of Joshua, Moses' successor.
event_year_calculation: The year of Joshua_1's birth is the year of the Exodus (2515) - his age at the time of the Exodus (42) = 2473
event_notes:
This event is keyed off of the The_Exodus event.
 
*EXTRA-BIBLICAL SOURCE:
Eliezer Shulman's "The Sequence of Events in the Old Testament" (page 107) places Joshua's birth in the year 2406 AM by working backwards from Joshua's death 14 years (judging while the Tent of Meeting was in Shiloh), 14 years (conquering and apportioning the Land), 40 years (wandering in the wilderness) to the Exodus when Joshua was 42 years old.
event_description_id: Kelahiran Yosua, penerus Musa.

event_description:
Now it came about in those days, when Moses had grown up, that he went out to his brethren and looked on their hard labors; and he saw an Egyptian beating a Hebrew, one of his brethren. When Pharaoh heard of this matter, he tried to kill Moses. But Moses fled from the presence of Pharaoh and settled in the land of Midian, and he sat down by a well. (EXO 2:11, 15)

But when he was approaching the age of FORTY, it entered his mind to visit his brethren, the sons of Israel. And when he saw one of them being treated unjustly, he defended him and took vengeance for the oppressed by striking down the Egyptian. (ACT 7:23-24)
event_year_calculation: The year Moses_1 fled from Pharaoh is Moses_1's birth year (2435) + his age when he fled (40) = 2475
event_notes:
Moses was almost 40 when he fled Egypt.
[Ussher date p34]
event_description_id:
Pada waktu itu, ketika Musa sudah dewasa, dia pergi menemui saudara-saudaranya dan melihat kerja keras mereka; dan dia melihat seorang Mesir memukuli seorang Ibrani, salah satu saudaranya. Ketika Firaun mendengar hal ini, dia mencoba membunuh Musa. Tetapi Musa melarikan diri dari hadapan Firaun dan menetap di tanah Midian, dan dia duduk di dekat sebuah sumur. (Kel. 2:11, 15)

Namun ketika ia mendekati usia empat puluh tahun, terlintas dalam pikirannya untuk mengunjungi saudara-saudaranya, bani Israel. Dan ketika dia melihat salah satu dari mereka diperlakukan tidak adil, dia membelanya dan membalas dendam kepada yang tertindas dengan membunuh orang Mesir itu. (Kisah 7:23-24)

event_description: I was FORTY YEARS old when Moses the servant of the LORD sent me from Kadesh-barnea to spy out the land, and I brought word back to him as it was in my heart. (JOS 14:7)
event_year_calculation: The year of Caleb_1's birth is the year the twelve spies were sent into the Land (2517) - his age at that time (40) = 2477
event_notes:
Caleb was 40 years old when he was sent to spy out the Land, two years after the Exodus.
* Shulman also has Caleb's birth four years after Joshua's.
[Shulman date p107]
[Ussher date p35]
event_description_id: Aku berumur EMPAT PULUH TAHUN ketika Musa, hamba TUHAN, mengutus aku dari Kadesh-Barnea untuk mengintai negeri itu, dan aku menyampaikan kabar kepadanya seperti yang ada dalam hatiku. (YOS 14:7)

event_name: End of the 400 years of oppression
event_description: God said to Abram, "Know for certain that your descendants will be strangers in a land that is not theirs, where they will be enslaved and oppressed four hundred years. (GEN 15:13)
event_year_calculation: The year the 400-year period of oppression ended is the year of the Exodus = 2515
event_notes: [Shulman date p45]
event_description_id: Allah berfirman kepada Abram, “Ketahuilah dengan pasti, bahwa keturunanmu akan menjadi orang asing di negeri yang bukan miliknya, di mana mereka akan diperbudak dan ditindas selama empat ratus tahun. (GEN 15:13)

event_description: Now the time that the sons of Israel lived in Egypt was FOUR HUNDRED AND THIRTY YEARS.  And at the end of four hundred and thirty years, to the very day, all the hosts of the LORD went out from the land of Egypt. (EXO 12:40-41)
event_year_calculation: The year of the Exodus is the year of Abram was called (2085) + the years until the covenant (430) = 2515
event_notes:
The Israelites (descendants of Abraham) were in Egypt AND THE LAND OF CANAAN for 430 years (Rahlf's Septuaginta and the Samaritan Pentateuch agree on this language in Exodus 12:40.)

This date MUST match the 400 years of oppression noted in Abram's promise. (GEN 15:13)
This date MUST ALSO match the 430 years between the Promise to Abram and the Covenant at Sinai (as Paul notes in Galatians 3:16-17)
 ** THIS DATE IS CRITICAL TO CALCULATING SEVERAL OTHER DATES. **
[Shulman date p45]
[Ussher date p39]
event_description_id: Masa tinggal bani Israil di Mesir adalah EMPAT RATUS TIGA PULUH TAHUN.  Dan pada akhir empat ratus tiga puluh tahun, sampai pada hari itu, seluruh pasukan TUHAN keluar dari tanah Mesir. (Kel. 12:40-41)

event_name: Israel began wandering in the wilderness
event_description:
The sons of Israel ate the manna FORTY YEARS, until they came to an inhabited land; they ate the manna until they came to the border of the land of Canaan. (EXO 16:35)
 
'Your sons shall be shepherds for forty years in the wilderness, and they will suffer for your unfaithfulness, until your corpses lie in the wilderness. 'According to the number of days which you spied out the land, forty days, for every day you shall bear your guilt a year, even FORTY YEARS, and you will know My opposition. (NUM 14:33-34)
 
"So the LORD'S anger burned against Israel, and He made them wander in the wilderness FORTY YEARS, until the entire generation of those who had done evil in the sight of the LORD was destroyed. (NUM 32:13)
 
For the sons of Israel walked FORTY YEARS in the wilderness, until all the nation, that is, the men of war who came out of Egypt, perished because they did not listen to the voice of the LORD, to whom the LORD had sworn that He would not let them see the land which the LORD had sworn to their fathers to give us, a land flowing with milk and honey. (JOS 5:6)
event_year_calculation: The year Israel's time of wandering in the wilderness began is the year of the Exodus = 2515
event_notes:
This event is keyed off of the The_Exodus event.
The Exodus 16:35 passage connects the beginning of the forty years with year they began eating mannah shortly after the Exodus.
[Shulman date p93]
[Ussher date p39]
peristiwa: Israel mulai mengembara di padang gurun
event_description_id:
Orang Israel memakan manna SELAMA EMPAT PULUH TAHUN, sampai mereka tiba di suatu negeri yang berpenduduk; mereka makan manna sampai mereka tiba di perbatasan tanah Kanaan. (Kel. 16:35)
 
'Anak-anakmu akan menjadi gembala selama empat puluh tahun di padang gurun, dan mereka akan menderita karena ketidaksetiaanmu, sampai mayatmu tergeletak di padang gurun. 'Sesuai dengan jumlah hari yang kamu mata-matai di negeri itu, empat puluh hari, karena setiap hari kamu akan menanggung kesalahanmu selama satu tahun, bahkan EMPAT PULUH TAHUN, dan kamu akan mengetahui perlawanan-Ku. (BIL. 14:33-34)
 
Maka berkobarlah murka TUHAN terhadap Israel, lalu dibuatnya mereka mengembara di padang gurun EMPAT PULUH TAHUN, sampai binasalah seluruh generasi orang-orang yang berbuat jahat di mata TUHAN itu. (Bil 32:13)
 
Sebab orang Israel berjalan SELAMA PULUH TAHUN di padang gurun, sampai seluruh bangsa, yaitu prajurit yang keluar dari Mesir, binasa karena mereka tidak mendengarkan suara TUHAN, kepada siapa TUHAN telah bersumpah bahwa Dia tidak akan membiarkan mereka melihat negeri yang telah disumpah TUHAN kepada nenek moyang mereka untuk diberikan kepada kita, negeri yang berlimpah susu dan madu. (Yos 5:6)

event_description: Now it came about in the FOUR HUNDRED AND EIGHTIETH YEAR after the sons of Israel came out of the land of Egypt, in the FOURTH YEAR of Solomon’s reign over Israel, in the month of Ziv which is the second month, that he began to build the house of the LORD. (1KI 6:1)
event_year_calculation: The year the 480-year Exodus-to-the-Temple period began is the year of the Exodus = 2515
event_notes: [Shulman date p115]
peristiwa: Periode Keluaran ke Bait Suci selama 480 tahun dimulai
event_description_id: Maka terjadilah pada TAHUN EMPAT RATUS DELAPAN PULUH setelah orang Israel keluar dari tanah Mesir, pada TAHUN KEEMPAT pemerintahan Salomo atas Israel, pada bulan Ziw, yaitu bulan yang kedua, barulah ia mulai membangun rumah TUHAN. (1KI 6:1)

event_name: G-d makes a national covenant with Israel at Sinai
event_description: ‘Now then, if you will indeed obey My voice and keep My covenant, then you shall be My own possession among all the peoples, for all the earth is Mine; and you shall be to Me a kingdom of priests and a holy nation.’ These are the words that you shall speak to the sons of Israel." So Moses came and called the elders of the people, and set before them all these words which the LORD had commanded him. All the people answered together and said, "All that the LORD has spoken we will do!" And Moses brought back the words of the people to the LORD. (EXO 19:5-8)
event_year_calculation: The year the LORD covenants with Israel at Sinai is the year of the Exodus = 2515.  Also, it was Isaac_1's birth year (2110) + years until he was weaned (5) + the years of oppression (400) = 2515
event_notes:
[Shulman date p55]
[Ussher date p40]
peristiwa: Tuhan membuat perjanjian nasional dengan Israel di Sinai
event_description_id: ‘Kalau begitu, jika kamu sungguh-sungguh mendengarkan firman-Ku dan menaati perjanjian-Ku, maka kamu akan menjadi milik-Ku di antara segala bangsa, karena seluruh bumi adalah milik-Ku; dan bagi-Ku kamu akan menjadi suatu kerajaan imam dan bangsa yang kudus.’ Inilah firman yang harus kamu ucapkan kepada orang Israel.” Maka datanglah Musa dan memanggil para tua-tua bangsa itu, dan menyampaikan di hadapan mereka semua firman yang diperintahkan TUHAN kepadanya. Seluruh bangsa itu menjawab bersama-sama dan berkata, “Segala yang difirmankan TUHAN akan kami lakukan!” Lalu Musa membawa kembali perkataan bangsa itu kepada TUHAN. (EXO 19:5-8)

event_name: End of the 430 years between the promise and the covenant
event_description: Now the promises were spoken to Abraham and to his seed. He does not say, "And to seeds," as referring to many, but rather to one, "And to your seed," that is, Christ. What I am saying is this: the Law, which came FOUR HUNDRED AND THIRTY YEARS later, does not invalidate a covenant previously ratified by God, so as to nullify the promise.  (GAL 3:16-17)
event_year_calculation: The year the 430-year period ended is the year the covenant is given at Sinai = 2515
event_notes:
[Shulman date p55]
[Ussher date p40]
peristiwa: Akhir dari 430 tahun antara janji dan perjanjian
event_description_id: Sekarang janji-janji itu telah disampaikan kepada Abraham dan keturunannya. Ia tidak berkata, “Dan kepada benih-benihmu,” yang merujuk pada banyak orang, melainkan kepada satu benih, “Dan kepada benihmu,” yaitu Kristus. Yang ingin saya katakan adalah: Hukum, yang muncul EMPAT RATUS TIGA PULUH TAHUN kemudian, tidak membatalkan perjanjian yang telah disahkan oleh Tuhan sebelumnya, sehingga dapat membatalkan janji tersebut.  (GAL 3:16-17)

event_name: Israel's wandering in the wilderness ended
event_description: Now it came about after the death of Moses the servant of the LORD, that the LORD spoke to Joshua the son of Nun, Moses' servant, saying, "Moses My servant is dead; now therefore arise, cross this Jordan, you and all this people, to the land which I am giving to them, to the sons of Israel. (JOS 1:1-2)

event_notes:
The Israelites forty-year period of wandering included the two years at Sinai building the tabernacle.  It is in that second year that the twelve men are sent to spy out the Land (NUM 13:1).  Moses specifies the thirty-eight year wandering period in Deuteronomy 2:14.

(JOS 1:1-2)

event_name: Israel's conquest of the Land of Canaan ended
event_description:
Therefore, Hebron became the inheritance of Caleb the son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite until this day, because he followed the LORD God of Israel fully.
Now the name of Hebron was formerly Kiriath-arba; for Arba was the greatest man among the Anakim. 
Then the land had rest from war. (JOS 14:14-15)
event_year_calculation: The year Israel's conquest of the Land ended is the year they entered the Land (2555) + 7 years of conquest = 2562
event_notes:
Caleb_1 was 40 years old (JOS 14:7) when he spied out the Land in 2517.
He was 85 at the end of the conquest in 2562.
The difference between the incident of the spies and the end of the conquest is 45 years.
Israel continued wandering in the wilderness for 38 of those years.
So, 45 - 38 = 7 years of conquest.
[Shulman date p107]
[Ussher date p52]
peristiwa: Penaklukan Israel atas Tanah Kanaan berakhir
event_description_id:
Itulah sebabnya Hebron menjadi milik pusaka Kaleb bin Yefune, orang Kenis itu sampai sekarang, karena ia mengikuti TUHAN, Allah Israel, dengan sepenuh hati.
Sekarang nama Hebron dulunya adalah Kiriath-arba; karena Arba adalah orang terhebat di antara suku Anakim. 
Kemudian negeri itu beristirahat dari perang. (JOS 14:14-15)

Now the lot for the tribe of the sons of Judah according to their families reached the border of Edom, southward to the wilderness of Zin at the extreme south. (JOS 15:1)

Pengundian suku bani Yehuda menurut kaum-kaum mereka sampai ke perbatasan Edom di sebelah selatan sampai ke padang gurun Zin di ujung paling selatan. (JOS 15:1)

[Levi had no portion in the Land (DEU 18:1) and Reuben, Gad, and half of Manasseh wanted land to the east of the Jordan. (NUM 32:1-5)]

event_name: Beginning of the period from the death of Joshua to the first judge*
event_description: So they forsook the LORD and served Baal and the Ashtaroth.  The anger of the LORD burned against Israel, and He gave them into the hands of plunderers who plundered them; and He sold them into the hands of their enemies around them, so that they could no longer stand before their enemies.  Then the LORD raised up judges who delivered them from the hands of those who plundered them. (JDG 2:13-14,16)
event_year_calculation: The year the Joshua-to-Judges period began is the year of Joshua_1's death = 2583
event_notes:
*EXTRA-BIBLICAL SOURCE:
Josephus' "Antiquities of the Jews" records a period of 18 years between Joshua and the first judge:
Antiq. 6:84 (6.5.4) for in the days of Moses and his disciple Joshua, who was their general, they continued under an aristocracy; but after the death of Joshua, for EIGHTEEN years in all, the multitude had no settled form of government, but were in an anarchy;
Antiq. 6:85 (6.5.4) after which they returned to their former government, they then permitted themselves to be judged by him who appeared to be the best warrior and most courageous, whence it was that they called this interval of their government the Judges.
peristiwa: Awal periode dari kematian Yosua hingga hakim pertama*
event_description_id: Maka mereka meninggalkan TUHAN dan mengabdi kepada Baal dan Asytarot.  Murka TUHAN berkobar terhadap Israel, dan Dia menyerahkan mereka ke dalam tangan para penjarah yang menjarah mereka; dan Dia menjual mereka ke tangan musuh-musuh di sekitar mereka, sehingga mereka tidak dapat lagi bertahan di hadapan musuh-musuhnya.  Lalu TUHAN membangkitkan hakim-hakim yang melepaskan mereka dari tangan penjarah. (JDG 2:13-14,16)

event_name: Beginning of the period of Israel's oppression by Cushan-rishathaim*
event_description: The sons of Israel did what was evil in the sight of the LORD, and forgot the LORD their God and served the Baals and the Asheroth.  Then the anger of the LORD was kindled against Israel, so that He sold them into the hands of Cushan-rishathaim king of Mesopotamia; and the sons of Israel served Cushan-rishathaim EIGHT years. (JDG 3:7-8)
event_year_calculation: The year Cushan-rishathaim's oppression began is the year his oppression ended (2601) - the years his oppression lasted (8) = 2593
event_notes: [Ussher date p52]
peristiwa: Awal masa penindasan Israel oleh Kushan-risyataim*
event_description_id: Orang Israel melakukan apa yang jahat di mata TUHAN, melupakan TUHAN, Allah mereka, dan beribadah kepada Baal dan Aserot.  Maka berkobarlah murka TUHAN terhadap Israel, sehingga Ia menjual mereka ke dalam tangan Kushan-risyataim, raja Mesopotamia; dan orang Israel mengabdi pada Kushan-risyataim DELAPAN tahun. (Hak 3:7-8)

event_name: Ending of the period from the death of Joshua to the first judge*
event_description: Then the LORD raised up judges who delivered them from the hands of those who plundered them. (JDG 2:16)
event_year_calculation: The year the Joshua-to-Judges period ended is the year it began (2583) + the elapsed years (18) = 2601
event_notes:
*EXTRA-BIBLICAL SOURCE:
Josephus' "Antiquities of the Jews" records a period of 18 years between Joshua and the first judge:
Antiq. 6:84 (6.5.4) for in the days of Moses and his disciple Joshua, who was their general, they continued under an aristocracy; but after the death of Joshua, for EIGHTEEN years in all, the multitude had no settled form of government, but were in an anarchy;
Antiq. 6:85 (6.5.4) after which they returned to their former government, they then permitted themselves to be judged by him who appeared to be the best warrior and most courageous, whence it was that they called this interval of their government the Judges.

event_description_id: Lalu TUHAN membangkitkan hakim-hakim yang melepaskan mereka dari tangan penjarah. (JDG 2:16)

event_name: Beginning of the period of Israel's oppression by Eglon*
event_description: Now the sons of Israel again did evil in the sight of the LORD. So the LORD strengthened Eglon the king of Moab against Israel, because they had done evil in the sight of the LORD. And he gathered to himself the sons of Ammon and Amalek; and he went and defeated Israel, and they possessed the city of the palm trees. The sons of Israel served Eglon the king of Moab EIGHTEEN years. (JDG 3:12-14)
event_year_calculation: The year Eglon_1's oppression of Israel began is the year Othniel_1 died = 2641
event_notes:
*HYPOTHESIS:
The year Eglon's oppression began is the year Othniel died.
[Ussher date p53]
peristiwa: Awal masa penindasan Israel oleh Eglon*
event_description_id: Kini orang Israel kembali melakukan apa yang jahat di mata TUHAN. Maka TUHAN menguatkan Eglon, raja Moab, terhadap Israel, karena mereka telah melakukan apa yang jahat di mata TUHAN. Dan dia mengumpulkan bagi dirinya sendiri anak-anak Amon dan Amalek; dan dia pergi dan mengalahkan Israel, dan mereka memiliki kota pohon palem. Bani Israil mengabdi pada Eglon, raja Moab, DELAPAN BELAS tahun. (Hak 3:12-14)

event_name: Beginning of the period of Israel's oppression by Jabin*
event_description: Then the sons of Israel again did evil in the sight of the LORD, after Ehud died.  And the LORD sold them into the hand of Jabin king of Canaan, who reigned in Hazor; and the commander of his army was Sisera, who lived in Harosheth-hagoyim.  The sons of Israel cried to the LORD; for he had nine hundred iron chariots, and he oppressed the sons of Israel severely for TWENTY years.  (JDG 4:1-3)
event_year_calculation: The year Jabin_1's oppression of Israel began is the year Ehud_1's time as judge ended (2659) - 10 years = 2649
event_notes:
*HYPOTHESIS:
The year Jabin's oppression began is ten years before Ehud's period of peace ended.
[Ussher date p53]
peristiwa: Awal masa penindasan Israel oleh Yabin*
event_description_id: Kemudian orang Israel kembali melakukan apa yang jahat di mata TUHAN, setelah Ehud mati.  Dan TUHAN menyerahkan mereka ke tangan Yabin, raja Kanaan, yang memerintah di Hazor; dan panglima pasukannya adalah Sisera, yang tinggal di Haroset-hagoyim.  Bani Israil berseru kepada TUHAN; karena dia mempunyai sembilan ratus kereta besi, dan dia menindas bani Israel dengan kejam selama DUA PULUH tahun.  (JDG 4:1-3)

event_description:
Now after Abimelech died, Tola the son of Puah, the son of Dodo, a man of Issachar, arose to save Israel; and he lived in Shamir in the hill country of Ephraim.
He judged Israel TWENTY-THREE years. Then he died and was buried in Shamir.  (JDG 10:1-2)
event_year_calculation: The year Tola_2 began to judge is the year Abimelech_2's rule ended = 2844
event_notes:

The year Tola began to judge is the year Abimelech's rule ended.
[Shulman date p83]
[Ussher date p54]
peristiwa: Awal masa Tola sebagai hakim Israel*
event_description_id:
Sekarang setelah Abimelekh meninggal, Tola bin Puah bin Dodo, seorang lelaki Isakhar, bangkit untuk menyelamatkan Israel; dan dia tinggal di Shamir di daerah perbukitan Efraim.
Dia menghakimi Israel DUA PULUH TIGA tahun. Kemudian dia meninggal dan dimakamkan di Shamir.  (Hak 10:1-2)

event_description: After him, Jair the Gileadite arose and judged Israel TWENTY-TWO years.  (JDG 10:1-3)

event_description_id: Setelah dia, Yair, orang Gilead, bangkit dan menghakimi Israel DUA PULUH DUA tahun.  (Hak 10:1-3)

event_description: Then Jephthah went with the elders of Gilead, and the people made him head and chief over them; and Jephthah spoke all his words before the LORD at Mizpah. (JDG 11:11)

event_notes:

*HYPOTHESIS:
The year Jephthah began to judge is the year Jair's time as judge ended.
[Shulman date p83]
[Ussher date p55]
peristiwa: Awal mula penghakiman Yefta terhadap Israel
event_description_id: Kemudian Yefta pergi bersama para tua-tua Gilead, dan rakyat mengangkat dia menjadi kepala dan kepala atas mereka; dan Yefta menyampaikan seluruh perkataannya di hadapan TUHAN di Mizpa. (Hak 11:11)

event_description: While Israel lived in Heshbon and its villages, and in Aroer and its villages, and in all the cities that are on the banks of the Arnon, THREE HUNDRED YEARS, why did you not recover them within that time? (JDG 11:26)
event_year_calculation: The year Jephthah_1's 300-year period ended is the year Israel enters the Land (2555) + 300 years = 2855
event_notes:
Although Israel conquered Heshbon and its villages at the time of the Exodus (NUM 21:25), they wandered for 40 years (NUM 32:13) and did not begin living there until they settled the Land.
[Shulman date p109]
[Ussher date p55]
peristiwa: Masa pemerintahan Yefta selama 300 tahun berakhir
event_description_id: Sementara Israel tinggal di Hesybon dan desa-desanya, dan di Aroer dan desa-desanya, dan di semua kota di tepi sungai Arnon, TIGA RATUS TAHUN, mengapa kamu tidak memulihkannya dalam waktu itu? (Hak 11:26)

event_description: Then the sons of Israel again did evil in the sight of the LORD, served the Baals and the Ashtaroth, the gods of Aram, the gods of Sidon, the gods of Moab, the gods of the sons of Ammon, and the gods of the Philistines; thus they forsook the LORD and did not serve Him.  The anger of the LORD burned against Israel, and He sold them into the hands of the Philistines and into the hands of the sons of Ammon.  (JDG 10:6-7)
event_year_calculation: The year the Philistine's and Ammonite's oppression of Israel began is the year Jair_2's time as judge began (2852) + 8 years = 2860

event_description: Now Barzillai the Gileadite had come down from Rogelim; and he went on to the Jordan with the king to escort him over the Jordan. The king said to Barzillai, “You cross over with me and I will sustain you in Jerusalem with me.” (2SA 19:31-33)

Barzilai sudah sangat tua, yaitu DELAPAN PULUH TAHUN; dan dia telah mendukung raja ketika dia tinggal di Mahanaim, karena dia adalah orang yang sangat hebat.  Raja berkata kepada Barzilai, “Seberangilah bersamaku dan aku akan mendukungmu di Yerusalem bersamaku.” (2SA 19:31-33)

event_description_id: Isyboset, anak Saul, berumur EMPAT PULUH TAHUN ketika ia menjadi raja atas Israel, dan ia menjadi raja selama DUA TAHUN. Namun kaum Yehuda mengikuti Daud. (2Sam 2:10)

event_name: Beginning of Samuel's time as judge of Israel*
event_description: They gathered to Mizpah, and drew water and poured it out before the LORD, and fasted on that day and said there, “We have sinned against the LORD.” And Samuel judged the sons of Israel at Mizpah. (1SA 7:6)
event_year_calculation: The year Samuel_2 began to judge is the year his time as judge ended (2949) - the 32 years he judged = 2917
event_notes:
*EXTRA-BIBLICAL SOURCE:
Josephus records that Samuel judged for THIRTY-TWO YEARS.  Since Saul's reign took up twelve of those thirty-two years, then Samuel's time as a judge before the reign of Saul would have taken up the remaining twenty.  Samuel's remaining time as judge would have overlapped Saul's time as king for ten of Saul's twelve-year reign.
[Shulman date p83]

event_name: The Philistines capture the ark in battle
event_description: So the Philistines fought and Israel was defeated, and every man fled to his tent; and the slaughter was very great, for there fell of Israel thirty thousand foot soldiers.  And the ark of God was taken; and the two sons of Eli, Hophni and Phinehas, died. (1SA 4:10-11)
event_year_calculation: The year the Philistines captured the ark is the year it was returned (2937) - 1 = 2936
event_notes:
The ark was with the Phililstines in Ashdod, Gath, and Ekron for seven months (1SA 6:1) before it was returned to the Israelites during the wheat harvest (1SA 6:13) which is usually around May/June.  The battle when the Philistines captured the ark (1SA 4:11) would have been towards the end of autumn (Nov/Dec) the year before.
[Ussher date p57]

event_description: The men of Beth-shemesh said, "Who is able to stand before the LORD, this holy God? And to whom shall He go up from us?"  So they sent messengers to the inhabitants of Kiriath-jearim, saying, "The Philistines have brought back the ark of the LORD; come down and take it up to you." (1SA 6:20-21)

peristiwa: Tabut itu berada di Bet-Semes setelah dikembalikan oleh orang Filistin*
event_description_id: Kata orang-orang Bet-Semes: "Siapakah yang sanggup berdiri di hadapan TUHAN, Allah yang kudus ini? Dan kepada siapakah Dia akan pergi meninggalkan kita?"  Maka mereka mengirim utusan kepada penduduk Kiriat-Yearim dengan pesan, “Orang Filistin telah membawa kembali tabut TUHAN; turunlah dan bawalah itu kepadamu.” (1Sam 6:20-21)

event_description: David said to Saul, “Let no man’s heart fail on account of him; your servant will go and fight with this Philistine.”  Then Saul said to David, “You are not able to go against this Philistine to fight with him; for you are but a youth while he has been a warrior from his youth.” (1SA 17:32-33)
event_year_calculation: The year David slew Goliath is the year David was born (2921) + his (estimated) age when it occurred (17) = 2938
event_notes:
*HYPOTHESIS:
Military age for Israelites was twenty years old (NUM 1:2-3).  David was not yet twenty years old or else he would have been a soldier in the military.  David is called a "youth" (נַעַר - na'ar) but he could not have been an adolescent or else he would not have been able to wear Saul's armor before the battle (1SA 17:38).  He was likely about 15-19 years old and I am using 17 as the mid-point in that range.  This is the also year after the ark was returned from the Philistines which may have strengthened David's courage.  If it was after the ark was captured but before it was returned he might not have been as courageous. (pure speculation)
event_description_id: Daud berkata kepada Saul, “Janganlah ada orang yang patah hati karena dia; hambamu ini akan pergi berperang melawan orang Filistin itu.”  Lalu berkatalah Saul kepada Daud: "Engkau tidak dapat melawan orang Filistin itu untuk berperang bersamanya; karena engkau masih muda, sedangkan ia telah menjadi pejuang sejak masa mudanya." (1Sam 17:32-33)

event_name: The ark is moved to Kiriath-jearim from Beth-shemesh
event_description: And the men of Kiriath-jearim came and took the ark of the LORD and brought it into the house of Abinadab on the hill, and consecrated Eleazar his son to keep the ark of the LORD. (1SA 7:1)
event_year_calculation: The year beginning the time the ark was in Kiriath-jearim (K-j) is the year David moved it to Jerusalem (2958) - the years it was in K-j (20) = 2938

peristiwa: Tabut dipindahkan ke Kiriat-Yearim dari Bet-Semes
event_description_id: Lalu orang-orang Kiriat-Yearim datang dan mengambil tabut TUHAN itu dan membawanya ke rumah Abinadab di atas bukit, lalu menguduskan Eleazar, anaknya, untuk menjaga tabut TUHAN itu. (1SA 7:1)

event_description: Saul was ? years old when he began to reign, and he reigned two years over Israel. (1SA 13:1)
event_year_calculation: The year Saul's reign began is the year his reign ended (2951) - the 12 years he reigned = 2939
event_notes:
*HYPOTHESIS:
Saul reigned for 12 years beginning the year AFTER the ark is returned to Kiriath-jearim and Israel asks for a king (see 1SA 8:5)
This article explains why his reign can only be 12 years: https://onemessianicgentile.com/articles/musings/the-age-and-reign-of-king-saul.html
[Shulman date p83]
[Ussher date p57]
event_description_id: Saulus itu? berumur dua tahun pada waktu ia mulai memerintah, dan dua tahun lamanya ia memerintah Israel. (1Sam 13:1)

event_description: Now Jonathan, Saul’s son, had a son crippled in his feet. He was FIVE YEARS old when the report of Saul and Jonathan came from Jezreel, and his nurse took him up and fled. And it happened that in her hurry to flee, he fell and became lame. And his name was Mephibosheth. (2SA 4:4)

event_description_id: Sekarang Yonatan, anak Saul, mempunyai seorang anak laki-laki yang cacat kakinya. Dia berumur LIMA TAHUN ketika laporan tentang Saul dan Yonatan datang dari Yizreel, dan perawatnya membawanya dan melarikan diri. Dan kebetulan ketika dia terburu-buru melarikan diri, dia terjatuh dan menjadi timpang. Dan namanya adalah Mefiboset. (2Sam 4:4)

event_name: End of Samuel's time as judge of Israel
event_description: Then Samuel died; and all Israel gathered together and mourned for him, and buried him at his house in Ramah. And David arose and went down to the wilderness of Paran. (1SA 25:1)
event_year_calculation: The year Samuel_2 ceased to judge Israel is 2 years* before Saul died (2951 - 2) = 2949
event_notes:
*EXTRA-BIBLICAL SOURCE:
Josephus records that Samuel judged for THIRTY-TWO YEARS.  Since Saul's reign took up twelve of those thirty-two years, then Samuel's time as a judge before the reign of Saul would have taken up the remaining twenty.  Samuel's remaining time as judge would have overlapped Saul's time as king for ten of Saul's twelve-year reign.
[Shulman date p83]

peristiwa: Absalom menyangkal wajah Raja Daud berakhir*
event_description_id: Maka ketika Yoab datang menghadap raja dan memberitahukannya, dia memanggil Absalom. Maka ia mendatangi raja dan bersujud di hadapan raja, lalu raja mencium Absalom. (2SA 14:33)

event_description: Now it came about at the end of FORTY YEARS that Absalom said to the king, “Please let me go and pay my vow which I have vowed to the LORD, in Hebron. (2SA 15:7)
event_year_calculation: The year of Absalom_1's rebellion is the year David slew Goliath (2938) + the 40 year period = 2978
event_notes:
*HYPOTHESIS:
The "forty years" of 2SA 15:7 could NOT have started with David's birth because Absalom would have only been an infant.
The "forty years" could NOT have started with David's reign because he only reigned forty years and this event occurred towards the *middle* of his reign, not the *end*.
The verse preceding the "forty years" sentence states that "Absalom stole away the hearts of the men of Israel." (2SA 15:6)  He stole the hearts of Israel away from his father David.  "All Israel and Judah loved David" (1SA 18:16) shortly after David slew Goliath.
I use this event as the start for the forty year period.
[Shulman date p119]

event_description_id: Pada akhir masa EMPAT PULUH TAHUN, Absalom berkata kepada raja: "Izinkanlah aku pergi dan membayar nazarku yang telah kujanjikan kepada TUHAN di Hebron." (2SA 15:7)

event_name: David fled Jerusalem as Absalom rebels
event_description: Then a messenger came to David, saying, “The hearts of the men of Israel are with Absalom.” David said to all his servants who were with him at Jerusalem, “Arise and let us flee, for otherwise none of us will escape from Absalom.  Go in haste, or he will overtake us quickly and bring down calamity on us and strike the city with the edge of the sword.” (2SA 15:13-14)

event_notes: [Ussher date p62]

event_description_id: Kemudian datanglah seorang utusan kepada Daud dan berkata, “Hati orang Israel tertuju pada Absalom.” Kata Daud kepada semua hambanya yang bersamanya di Yerusalem, "Bangunlah dan marilah kita melarikan diri, karena jika tidak, tidak ada seorang pun di antara kita yang akan luput dari Absalom. Pergilah secepatnya, atau dia akan segera menyusul kita dan mendatangkan malapetaka atas kita dan memukul kota itu dengan ujung pedang." (2SA 15:13-14)

event_description: The king charged Joab and Abishai and Ittai, saying, “Deal gently for my sake with the young man Absalom.” And aall the people heard when the king charged all the commanders concerning Absalom. Then the people went out into the field against Israel, and the battle took place in the forest of Ephraim. (2SA 18:5-6)
event_year_calculation: The year of the battle of the forest of Ephraim is the year Absalom rebelled = 2978

Kemudian bangsa itu pergi ke padang melawan Israel, dan terjadilah pertempuran di hutan Efraim. (2SA 18:5-6)

event_description: Then Joab said, “I will not waste time here with you.” So he took three spears in his hand and thrust them through the heart of Absalom while he was yet alive in the midst of the oak. event_name: Ahijah the Shilonite's first prophecy to Jereboam*
event_description: It came about at that time, when Jeroboam went out of Jerusalem, that the prophet Ahijah the Shilonite found him on the road. Now Ahijah had clothed himself with a new cloak; and both of them were alone in the field.  Then Ahijah took hold of the new cloak which was on him and tore it into twelve pieces.  He said to Jeroboam, “Take for yourself ten pieces; for thus says the LORD, the God of Israel, ‘Behold, I will tear the kingdom out of the hand of Solomon and give you ten tribes.'" (1KI 11:29-31)
event_year_calculation: The year of Ahijah's first prophecy to Jeroboam is estimated to be the year of Solomon's death (3031) - 5 years = 3026
event_notes:
*HYPOTHESIS:
Five years before Solomon's death is sufficient time in his life to obtain "seven hundred wives, princesses, and three hundred concubines" (1KI 11:3) and for them to "turn his heart away" from the LORD.  It also allows sufficient time for his deep introspection and repentance which he recorded in the book of Ecclesiastes.

Kemudian Ahia memegang jubah baru yang dikenakannya dan merobeknya menjadi dua belas bagian.  Katanya kepada Yerobeam: "Ambillah sepuluh keping bagimu; sebab beginilah firman TUHAN, Allah Israel, 'Sesungguhnya, Aku akan merenggut kerajaan itu dari tangan Salomo dan memberikan kepadamu sepuluh suku.'" (1KI 11:29-31)

event_name: Ahijah the Shilonite's second prophecy to Jereboam*
event_description: Go, say to Jeroboam, ‘Thus says the LORD God of Israel, “Because I exalted you from among the people and made you leader over My people Israel, and tore the kingdom away from the house of David and gave it to you — yet you have not been like My servant David, who kept My commandments and who followed Me with all his heart, to do only that which was right in My sight; you also have done more evil than all who were before you, and have gone and made for yourself other gods and molten images to provoke Me to anger, and have cast Me behind your back — therefore behold, I am bringing calamity on the house of Jeroboam, and will cut off from Jeroboam every male person, both bond and free in Israel, and I will make a clean sweep of the house of Jeroboam, as one sweeps away dung until it is all gone. (1KI 14:7-10)
event_year_calculation: The year of Ahijah's second prophecy to Jeroboam is estimated to be the year Jereboam's reign began (3031) + 5 years = 3036
event_notes:
*HYPOTHESIS:
Five years after Jereboam's reign began is sufficient time for him to institute his idolatrous practices (1KI 12), be warned (1KI 13), and fail to repent (1KI 13:33), resulting in Ahijah's second prophecy.
[Shulman date p83]

event_name: Azariah son of Oded prophesied to Asa*
event_description: Now the Spirit of God came on Azariah the son of Oded, and he went out to meet Asa and said to him, “Listen to me, Asa, and all Judah and Benjamin: the LORD is with you when you are with Him. And if you seek Him, He will let you find Him; but if you forsake Him, He will forsake you. (1CH 15:1-2)
event_year_calculation: The year Azariah son of Oded prophesied to Asa is the year of Judah's celebration (3068) - 1 year = 3067
event_notes:
*HYPOTHESIS:
Azariah prophesies about ONE YEAR BEFORE Judah's celebration.  This allows time for Asa to remove the idols, restore the altar of the LORD, and prepare for the celebration.
peristiwa: Azarya bin Oded bernubuat kepada Asa*
event_description_id: Lalu Roh Allah turun ke atas Azariah bin Oded, lalu keluarlah ia menyongsong Asa dan berkata kepadanya: "Dengarkan aku, Asa, dan seluruh Yehuda dan Benyamin: TUHAN menyertai kamu, bila kamu bersama-Nya. Dan jika kamu mencari Dia, Dia akan membiarkan kamu menemukan Dia; tetapi jika kamu meninggalkan Dia, Dia akan meninggalkan kamu. (1CH 15:1-2)

event_name: Asa tears down idols and Judah enters into the covenant
event_description: So they assembled at Jerusalem in the third month of the FIFTEENTH YEAR of Asa’s reign.  They sacrificed to the LORD that day 700 oxen and 7,000 sheep from the spoil they had brought.  They entered into the covenant to seek the LORD God of their fathers with all their heart and soul; (2CH 15:10-12)
event_year_calculation: The year Asa tears down idols and Judah enters into the covenant (3068) is the year his reign began (3052) + the year of his reign (15) + the 1-year accession year duration undercount = 3068
event_notes:
*HYPOTHESIS:
Judah began counting Asa's regnal years in AH 3053 instead of AH 3052 due to their "accession year" methodology.  See dataset notes for more information.

event_name: Tibni's reign as king over Israel ends*
event_description: But the people who followed Omri prevailed over the people who followed Tibni the son of Ginath. And Tibni died and Omri became king. (1KI 16:22)
event_year_calculation: The year Tibni's reign ended is the year his reign began (3078) + the length of his reign (6) - the 1-year accession/non-accession-year offset - Israel's 1-year all-calendar-years overcount = 3082
event_notes:
*HYPOTHESIS:
Tibni and Omri began a disputed reign over Israel at the same time.  Omri is stated to have ruled TWELVE years (total) with SIX undisputed in Tirzah.  This leaves SIX years of his reign disputed with Omri.  Also, Israel and Judah counted the years of a king's reign differently than the other and also began their calendar year on different days.  These two factors need to be considered when calculating this date.  See dataset notes for more information.

event_description: But the people who followed Omri prevailed over the people who followed Tibni the son of Ginath. And Tibni died and Omri became king. (1KI 16:22)

event_year_calculation: The year Omri's reign began was the year Zimri's reign ended = 3078.  However, His reign was disputed by those who supported Tibni_1. The year Omri's UNDISPUTED reign began is in year 31 of Asa's reign (3052 + 31) - the 1-year accession/non-accession-year offset = 3082

*HYPOTHESIS:

event_description_id: Elia, orang Tishbit, salah satu pemukim Gilead, berkata kepada Ahab, “Demi TUHAN, Allah Israel yang hidup, di hadapan-Nya aku berdiri, sesungguhnya tahun-tahun ini tidak akan ada embun atau hujan, kecuali dengan firmanku.” (1KI 17:1)

event_description: Jehoshaphat was a regent of Judah under his father, Asa, for four years.  This reconciles the ascension of his son, Jehoram, as king the year that he died and with it being the FIFTH YEAR of Joram, the son of Ahab (2KI 8:16).
event_year_calculation: The year Jehoshaphat's regency began is the year his reign ended (3114) - the 25 years of his reign + the 1-year accession year duration undercount = 3090
event_notes:
Consider also that Jehoshaphat's regency began in the 39th year of Asa and this verse:
In the THIRTY-NINTH YEAR of his reign Asa became diseased in his feet. His disease was severe, yet even in his disease he did not seek the LORD, but the physicians. (2CH 16:12)

event_description: Ahaziah was TWENTY-TWO YEARS old when he became king, and he reigned ONE YEAR in Jerusalem. And his mother’s name was Athaliah the granddaughter of Omri king of Israel. (2KI 8:26)

event_description: In his [Ahab's] days Hiel the Bethelite built Jericho; he laid its foundations with the loss of Abiram his firstborn, and set up its gates with the loss of his youngest son Segub, according to the word of the LORD, which He spoke by Joshua the son of Nun. (1KI 16:34)
event_year_calculation: The year Hiel_1 rebuilt Jericho is (approximately) the year Ahab's reign began (3088) + half of the length of his reign (11) = 3099
event_notes:
Curiously, Ussher omits Hiel in his text.
*HYPOTHESIS:
The passage states "In his days" referring to Ahab.  This is an approximate date placed in the middle of Ahab's reign.
peristiwa: Hiel, pekerja Betel membangun kembali Yerikho*
event_description_id: Pada zamannya [Ahab], Hiel, orang Betel, membangun Yerikho; ia meletakkan dasar-dasarnya dengan hilangnya anak sulung Abiram, dan mendirikan pintu-pintu gerbangnya dengan hilangnya Segub, anak bungsunya, sesuai dengan firman TUHAN yang diucapkan-Nya melalui Yosua bin Nun. (1KI 16:34)

event_description: The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, “There is yet one man by whom we may inquire of the LORD, but I hate him, because he does not prophesy good concerning me, but evil. He is Micaiah son of Imlah.” But Jehoshaphat said, “Let not the king say so.” (1KI 22:8)
event_year_calculation: The estimated year Micaiah_1's time as prophet begins is at least 1 year before the battle of Ramoth-gilead (3109) since Ahab complains of his former prophecies of evil against Ahab = 3108
event_notes:
*HYPOTHESIS:
Micaiah prophesies against Ahab at least one year before the prophecy regarding the battle at Ramoth-gilead.
event_description_id: Raja Israel berkata kepada Yosafat, "Masih ada satu orang yang dapat kita tanyakan kepada TUHAN, tetapi aku membenci dia, karena dia tidak menubuatkan hal yang baik tentang aku, melainkan hal yang buruk. Dialah Mikha bin Imlah." Namun Yosafat berkata, “Janganlah raja berkata demikian.” (1KI 22:8)

event_description: Jehoram was a regent of Judah under his father, Jehoshaphat.  This reconciles 2KI 1:17 and 2KI 3:1.  (see notes)
event_year_calculation: The year Jehoram's regency began is the year Jehoshaphat's reign began (3093) + 16 years = 3109
event_notes:
So Ahaziah died according to the word of the LORD which Elijah had spoken. And because he had no son, JEHORAM became king in his place in the SECOND YEAR of Jehoram the son of Jehoshaphat, king of Judah. (2KI 1:17)
Now JEHORAM the son of Ahab became king over Israel at Samaria in the EIGHTEENTH YEAR of Jehoshaphat king of Judah, and reigned twelve years.
THE ONLY WAY FOR THE SECOND YEAR OF JEHORAM AND THE EIGHTEENTH YEAR OF JEHOSHAPHAT TO BE THE SAME YEAR IS WITH JEHORAM BEING A REGENT DURING HIS FATHER'S REIGN.
[Shulman date p83]
[Ussher date p71]

event_description: Now the king of Israel [Ahab] said to his servants, “Do you know that Ramoth-gilead belongs to us, and we are still doing nothing to take it out of the hand of the king of Aram?”  And he said to Jehoshaphat, “Will you go with me to battle at Ramoth-gilead?” And Jehoshaphat said to the king of Israel, “I am as you are, my people as your people, my horses as your horses.” (1KI 22:3-4)
event_year_calculation: The year Ahab_1 fought at the battle of Ramoth-gilead is the same year he died and his reign ended = 3109

event_description: The LORD said to him, “Go, return on your way to the wilderness of Damascus, and when you have arrived, you shall anoint Hazael king over Aram; and Jehu the son of Nimshi you shall anoint king over Israel; and Elisha the son of Shaphat of Abel-meholah you shall anoint as prophet in your place.  So he departed from there and found Elisha the son of Shaphat, while he was plowing with twelve pairs of oxen before him, and he with the twelfth.  And Elijah passed over to him and threw his mantle on him. (1KI 19:15-16, 19)
event_year_calculation: The estimated year Elisha's time as prophet begins is the year Elijah's time as prophet ended = 3110

event_description_id: TUHAN berfirman kepadanya: "Pergilah, kembalilah ke padang gurun Damaskus, dan setelah kamu tiba, kamu harus mengurapi Hazael menjadi raja atas Aram; dan Yehu bin Nimsi harus kamu urapi menjadi raja atas Israel; dan Elisa bin Safat dari Abel-meholah harus kamu urapi sebagai nabi menggantikanmu. Maka berangkatlah dia dari sana dan menjumpai Elisa bin Safat, ketika dia sedang membajak dengan dua belas pasang lembu sebelumnya. dia, dan dia dengan yang kedua belas. event_description: So Ahaziah died according to the word of the LORD which Elijah had spoken. And because he had no son, Jehoram became king in his place in the second year of Jehoram the son of Jehoshaphat, king of Judah.  Now the rest of the acts of Ahaziah which he did, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel?  And it came about when the LORD was about to take up Elijah by a whirlwind to heaven, that Elijah went with Elisha from Gilgal.  (2KI 1:17-2:1)
event_year_calculation: The estimated year Elijah's time as prophet ends is the year Ahaziah died = 3110
event_notes:
Elijah's time of prophecy ended the year of Ahaziah's death

peristiwa: Masa Elia sebagai nabi telah berakhir
event_description_id: Maka Ahazia mati sesuai dengan firman TUHAN yang diucapkan Elia. Dan karena ia tidak mempunyai anak laki-laki, maka Yoram menjadi raja menggantikan dia pada tahun kedua pemerintahan Yoram bin Yosafat, raja Yehuda.  Nah, perbuatan Ahazia selebihnya yang dilakukannya, bukankah semuanya itu tertulis dalam Kitab Tawarikh Raja-Raja Israel?  Dan ketika TUHAN hendak mengangkat Elia ke surga dengan angin puyuh, berangkatlah Elia bersama Elisa dari Gilgal.  (2KI 1:17-2:1)

event_description: For thus says the LORD, ‘You shall not see wind nor shall you see rain; yet that valley shall be filled with water, so that you shall drink, both you and your cattle and your beasts. This is but a slight thing in the sight of the LORD; He will also give the Moabites into your hand.' (2KI 3:17-18)

Ini hanyalah suatu hal yang remeh di mata TUHAN; Dia juga akan menyerahkan orang Moab ke tanganmu.' (2KI 3:17-18)

event_name: Jehoram and Jehoshaphat battle against Moab
event_description: But when Ahab died, the king of Moab rebelled against the king of Israel.  And King Jehoram went out of Samaria at that time and mustered all Israel.  Then he went and sent word to Jehoshaphat the king of Judah, saying, “The king of Moab has rebelled against me. Will you go with me to fight against Moab?” And he said, “I will go up; I am as you are, my people as your people, my horses as your horses.” (2KI 3:5-7)
event_year_calculation: The year of Jehoram's and Jehoshaphat's battle against Moab is the year after Jehoram's reign began (3110 + , 1) = 3111

peristiwa: Yoram dan Yosafat berperang melawan Moab
event_description_id: Namun ketika Ahab meninggal, raja Moab memberontak melawan raja Israel.  Dan Raja Yoram pada waktu itu keluar dari Samaria dan mengumpulkan seluruh Israel.  Kemudian dia pergi dan mengirim pesan kepada Yosafat, raja Yehuda, katanya, "Raja Moab telah memberontak terhadapku. Maukah engkau ikut denganku berperang melawan Moab?" Dan dia berkata, “Aku akan pergi; aku sama seperti kamu, bangsaku seperti bangsamu, kudaku seperti kudamu.” (2KI 3:5-7)

event_description: Then a letter came to him from Elijah the prophet saying, "Thus says the LORD God of your father David, 'Because you have not walked in the ways of Jehoshaphat your father and the ways of Asa king of Judah, but have walked in the way of the kings of Israel, and have caused Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem to play the harlot as the house of Ahab played the harlot, and you have also killed your brothers, your own family, who were better than you, behold, the LORD is going to strike your people, your sons, your wives and all your possessions with a great calamity; and you will suffer severe sickness, a disease of your bowels, until your bowels come out because of the sickness, day by day.'" peristiwa: Pemerintahan Yoas (Yehoas) sebagai raja atas Israel berakhir*
event_description_id: Pada tahun ketiga puluh tujuh pemerintahan Yoas, raja Yehuda, Yoas bin Yoahas menjadi raja atas Israel di Samaria, dan memerintah ENAM BELAS TAHUN. (2KI 13:10)

event_description: In the FIFTEENTH YEAR of Amaziah the son of Joash king of Judah, Jeroboam the son of Joash king of Israel became king in Samaria, and reigned forty-one years. (2KI 14:23)
event_year_calculation: The year Jeroboam's co-reign with Joash_4 (Jehoash) ended is the year his reign ended = 3180
event_notes:
Amaziah died and his son, Azariah (Uzziah), became king in his place in the same year (3195) which was the TWENTY-SEVENTH YEAR of Jereboam (2KI 15:1).
Basic math (3195 - 27) would place the start of Jereboam's reign in 3168.
However, 2KI 14:23 states Jereboam became king in the FIFTEENTH YEAR of Amaziah (3166 + 15 - 1 year duration overcount = 3180).
HOW CAN IT BE BOTH?
The year Amaziah died (3187) was FIFTEEN YEARS *AFTER* the death of Jehoash (2KI 14:17) which means Jehoash' death occurred in 3187 - 15 = 3172 (math).
This tells us that Jereboah co-reigned with his father for TWELVE YEARS.

[Ussher date p73]

peristiwa: Pemerintahan Menachem sebagai raja atas Israel dimulai*

event_name: Pekah's rival reign with Menachem as king over Israel begins*
event_description: Pekah's rival reign with Menachem began in the THIRTY-NINTH YEAR of Azariah.
event_year_calculation: The year Pekah's rival reign with Menachem as king over Israel began is the same year as Menachem's reign began = 3210
event_notes:
*HYPOTHESIS:
Hoshea takes the throne in the 12th year of Ahaz (2KI 17:1) upon the death of Pekah.
The 12th year of Ahaz would have been the same as the 20th year of Jotham (math).
The only way for the END of Pekah's reign to align with the START of Hoshea's reign AND the START of Jotham's reign to align with the 2nd year of Pekah is if Pekah had a rival reign with Menachem that began in the same year as Menachem's reign.
His undisputed SOLE reign began in the 52nd year of Azariah (2KI 15:27).

event_notes:
"He was twenty-five years old when he became king" (2KI 15:33)
We know this was a co-regency because of the start date (second year of Pekah) is still during the reign of Azariah.

This is the "second year of Pekah" when counted using Israel's non-accession-year approach.
