event_description_id: Perkataan Yeremia bin Hilkia, salah satu imam di Anatot di tanah Benyamin, yang menerima firman TUHAN pada zaman Yosia bin Amon, raja Yehuda, pada TAHUN KETIGA BELAS pemerintahannya. (YER 1:1-2)

event_description: Now in the EIGHTEENTH YEAR of King Josiah, the king sent Shaphan, the son of Azaliah the son of Meshullam the scribe, to the house of the LORD saying,  “Go up to Hilkiah the high priest that he may count the money brought in to the house of the LORD which the doorkeepers have gathered from the people." (2KI 22:3-4)
event_year_calculation: The year Josiah began to repair the Temple (3339) is the year his reign began (3321) + the length of his reign to that point (18) = 3339
event_notes:
See also 2CH 34:8
[Ussher date p92]
event_description_id: Pada tahun DELAPAN BELAS pemerintahan Raja Yosia, raja mengutus Syafan bin Azalia bin Mesulam, ahli Taurat, ke rumah TUHAN sambil berkata, “Naiklah menghadap Imam Besar Hilkia agar dia dapat menghitung uang yang dibawa ke rumah TUHAN yang telah dikumpulkan oleh para penjaga pintu dari rakyat.” (2KI 22:3-4)

event_description: Then the king commanded all the people saying, “Celebrate the Passover to the LORD your God bas it is written in this book of the covenant.”  Surely such a Passover had not been celebrated from the days of the judges who judged Israel, nor in all the days of the kings of Israel and of the kings of Judah.  But in the EIGHTEENTH YEAR of King Josiah, this Passover was observed to the LORD in Jerusalem. (2KI 23:21-23)
event_year_calculation: The year of Josiah's Passover is the year his reign began (3321) + the year within his reign (18) = 3339
event_notes: [Ussher date p92]
event_description_id: Lalu raja memerintahkan seluruh rakyatnya sambil berkata, “Rayakanlah Paskah bagi TUHAN, Allahmu, seperti yang tertulis dalam kitab perjanjian ini.”  Tentu saja, Paskah seperti itu belum pernah dirayakan sejak zaman para hakim yang mengadili Israel, dan juga pada zaman raja-raja Israel dan raja-raja Yehuda.  Namun pada TAHUN KEDELAPAN BELAS pemerintahan Raja Yosia, Paskah ini diperingati bagi TUHAN di Yerusalem. (2KI 23:21-23)

event_description: Zedekiah was TWENTY-ONE YEARS old when he became king, and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem; and his mother’s name was Hamutal the daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah. (2KI 24:18)
event_year_calculation: The year of Mattaniah_1's (Zedekiah's) birth is the year his reign began (3365) - his age when his reign began (21) = 3344
event_notes: [Ussher date p93]
event_description_id: Zedekia berumur DUA PULUH SATU TAHUN ketika ia menjadi raja, dan ia memerintah selama sebelas tahun di Yerusalem; dan nama ibunya adalah Hamutal putri Yeremia dari Libna. (2KI 24:18)

event_description: Jehoiachin was EIGHTEEN YEARS old when he became king, and he reigned three months in Jerusalem; and his mother’s name was Nehushta the daughter of Elnathan of Jerusalem. (2KI 24:8)
event_year_calculation: The year of Jehoiachin_1's birth is the year his reign began (3364) - his age when his reign began (18) = 3346
event_notes: [Ussher date p93]
event_description_id: Yoyakhin berumur DELAPAN BELAS TAHUN ketika ia menjadi raja, dan ia memerintah selama tiga bulan di Yerusalem; dan nama ibunya adalah Nehushta putri Elnatan dari Yerusalem. (2KI 24:8)

event_description: The oracle of Nineveh. The book of the vision of Nahum the Elkoshite. (NAH 1:1)
event_year_calculation: The estimated year Nahum's time as prophet ends was the year before Nineveh was destroyed  (612 BCE) - 1 = 3349
event_notes:
[Shulman date p83]
Nahum's prophecy preceded the fall of Nineveh.
peristiwa: Masa Nahum sebagai Nabi telah berakhir*
event_description_id: Peramal Niniwe. Kitab visi Nahum si Elkoshite. (NAH 1:1)

event_description: The word of the LORD which came to Zephaniah son of Cushi, son of Gedaliah, son of Amariah, son of Hezekiah, in the days of Josiah son of Amon, king of Judah (ZEP 1:1)
event_year_calculation: The estimated year Zephaniah's time as prophet ends is the year Josiah's reign ended = 3353
event_notes: [Shulman date p83]
peristiwa: Masa Zefanya sebagai nabi telah berakhir*
event_description_id: Firman TUHAN yang datang kepada Zefanya bin Kusi bin Gedalya bin Amarya bin Hizkia pada zaman Yosia bin Amon raja Yehuda (ZEP 1:1)

event_name: Josiah's reign as king over Judah ends*
event_description: Josiah was eight years old when he became king, and he reigned THIRTY-ONE YEARS in Jerusalem; and his mother’s name was Jedidah the daughter of Adaiah of Bozkath. (2KI 22:1)
event_year_calculation: The year Josiah's reign ended is the year his reign began (3321) + the length of his reign (31) + 1 year for accession year undercount = 3353
event_notes:
*HYPOTHESIS:
Judah counted regnal years starting the year AFTER a king took the throne (accession year) resulting in a 1-year undercount.  See dataset notes for more information.
[Shulman date p83]
[Ussher date p94]
peristiwa: Pemerintahan Yosia sebagai raja atas Yehuda berakhir*
event_description_id: Yosia berumur delapan tahun ketika dia menjadi raja, dan dia memerintah TIGA PULUH SATU TAHUN di Yerusalem; dan nama ibunya adalah Jedidah putri Adaiah dari Bozkath. (2KI 22:1)

event_description: In his days Pharaoh Neco king of Egypt went up to the king of Assyria to the river Euphrates. And King Josiah went to meet him, and when Pharaoh Neco saw him he killed him at Megiddo. (2KI 23:29)
event_year_calculation: The year of Josiah_1's death (3353) is the year his reign ended = 3353
event_notes:
*HYPOTHESIS:
Josiah's death occurred when his reign ended
[Ussher date p94]
event_description_id: Pada zamannya Firaun Neko raja Mesir pergi menghadap raja Asyur ke sungai Efrat. Dan Raja Yosia pergi menemuinya, dan ketika Firaun Neko melihatnya, dia membunuhnya di Megido. (2KI 23:29)

event_name: Jehoahaz's reign as king over Judah begins
event_description: His servants drove his body in a chariot from Megiddo, and brought him to Jerusalem and buried him in his own tomb. Then the people of the land took Jehoahaz the son of Josiah and anointed him and made him king in place of his father. (2KI 23:30)
event_year_calculation: The year Jehoahaz's reign began is the year Josiah's reign ended = 3353
event_notes:
[Shulman date p83]
[Ussher date p94]
peristiwa: Pemerintahan Yoahaz sebagai raja atas Yehuda dimulai
event_description_id: Para hambanya mengendarai jenazahnya dengan kereta dari Megido, lalu membawanya ke Yerusalem dan menguburkannya di dalam kuburnya sendiri. Kemudian penduduk negeri itu mengambil Yoahaz bin Yosia, lalu mengurapi dia dan mengangkat dia menjadi raja menggantikan ayahnya. (2KI 23:30)

event_name: Jehoahaz's reign as king over Judah ends
event_description:
Jehoahaz was twenty-three years old when he became king, and he reigned THREE MONTHS in Jerusalem; and his mother’s name was Hamutal the daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah. (2KI 23:31)
Pharaoh Neco imprisoned him at Riblah in the land of Hamath, that he might not reign in Jerusalem; and he imposed on the land a fine of one hundred talents of silver and a talent of gold. (2KI 23:33)
event_year_calculation: The year Jehoahaz's reign ended is the year his reign began (3353) + the length of his reign (3 months) = 3353
event_notes:
[Shulman date p83]
[Ussher date p94]
peristiwa: Pemerintahan Yoahas sebagai raja atas Yehuda berakhir
event_description_id:
Yoahaz berumur dua puluh tiga tahun ketika dia menjadi raja, dan dia memerintah TIGA BULAN di Yerusalem; dan nama ibunya adalah Hamutal putri Yeremia dari Libna. (2KI 23:31)
Firaun Neko memenjarakannya di Ribla di tanah Hamat, agar ia tidak memerintah di Yerusalem; dan dia mengenakan denda seratus talenta perak dan satu talenta emas kepada negeri itu. (2KI 23:33)

event_name: Eliakim's (Jehoiakim's) reign as king over Judah begins
event_description: Pharaoh Neco made Eliakim the son of Josiah king in the place of Josiah his father, and changed his name to Jehoiakim. But he took Jehoahaz away and brought him to Egypt, and he died there. (2KI 23:34)
event_year_calculation: The year Eliakim's (Jehoiakim's) reign began is the year Jehoahaz's reign ended = 3353
event_notes:
[Shulman date p83]
[Ussher date p94]
peristiwa: Pemerintahan Eliakim (Yoyakim) sebagai raja atas Yehuda dimulai
event_description_id: Firaun Neko mengangkat Eliakim anak Yosia menjadi raja menggantikan Yosia ayahnya, dan mengubah namanya menjadi Yoyakim. Namun Yoahaz dibawanya pergi dan dibawanya ke Mesir, lalu ia mati di sana. (2KI 23:34)

event_description: For behold, I am araising up the Chaldeans, That fierce and impetuous people Who march throughout the earth To seize dwelling places which are not theirs. (HAB 1:6)
event_year_calculation: The estimated year Habakkuk's time as prophet begins is the year Eliakim's regin began = 3353
event_notes: [Ussher date p95]
event_description_id: Sebab lihatlah, Aku membangkitkan orang-orang Kasdim, yaitu bangsa yang ganas dan tidak sabaran, yang berbaris di seluruh bumi Untuk merebut tempat tinggal yang bukan milik mereka. (HAB 1:6)

event_name: Nebuchadnezzar's reign as king over Babylon begins
event_description: Jehoiachin the king of Judah went out to the king of Babylon, he and his mother and his servants and his captains and his officials. So the king of Babylon took him captive in the EIGHTH YEAR of his reign. (2KI 24:12)
event_year_calculation: The year Nebuchadnezzar's reign began is 8 years before Jehoiachin's reign ended (3365 - 8) = 3357
event_notes:
[Shulman date p142]
[Ussher date p95]
peristiwa: Pemerintahan Nebukadnezar sebagai raja Babel dimulai
event_description_id: Yoyakhin, raja Yehuda, pergi menemui raja Babel, ia beserta ibunya, para hambanya, para kaptennya, dan para pejabatnya. Maka raja Babel menawannya pada TAHUN KEDELAPAN pemerintahannya. (2KI 24:12)

event_description: In the THIRD YEAR of the reign of Jehoiakim king of Judah, Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came to Jerusalem and besieged it.  Then the king ordered Ashpenaz, the chief of his officials, to bring in some of the sons of Israel, including some of the royal afamily and of the nobles, youths in whom was no defect, who were good-looking, showing intelligence in every branch of wisdom, endowed with understanding and discerning knowledge, and who had ability for serving in the king’s court; and he ordered him to teach them the literature and language of the Chaldeans. Now among them from the sons of Judah were Daniel, Hananiah, Mishael and Azariah. (DAN 1:1, 3-6)
event_year_calculation: The year Daniel's captivity began is the year Jehoiakim's reign began (3353) + the year of his reign with the captivity began (3) + 1 year for accession year undercount = 3357
event_notes:
*HYPOTHESIS:
Daniel's count used the accession year counting of Jehoiakim's regnal years resulting in a 1-year undercount.  See dataset notes for more information.
[Shulman date p146]
[Ussher date p96]
event_description_id: Pada TAHUN KETIGA pemerintahan Yoyakim raja Yehuda, Nebukadnezar raja Babel datang ke Yerusalem dan mengepungnya.  Kemudian raja memerintahkan Aspenas, kepala para pejabatnya, untuk membawa masuk beberapa orang Israel, termasuk beberapa keluarga kerajaan dan para bangsawan, pemuda-pemuda yang tidak ada cacatnya, yang berpenampilan menarik, yang pandai dalam segala cabang kebijaksanaan, yang mempunyai pengertian dan pengetahuan yang tajam, dan yang mempunyai kesanggupan untuk mengabdi di istana raja; dan dia memerintahkan dia untuk mengajari mereka sastra dan bahasa orang Kasdim. Di antara mereka di antara bani Yehuda ada Daniel, Hananya, Mishael dan Azaria. (DAN 1:1, 3-6)

event_description: The word that came to Jeremiah concerning all the people of Judah, in the FOURTH YEAR of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah, king of Judah (that was the first year of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon), (JER 25:1)
event_year_calculation: The year Jeremiah prophesied about Nebuchadnezzar is the year Eliakim's reign began (3353) + 4 years = 3357
event_notes: [Ussher date p95]
event_description_id: Firman yang datang kepada Yeremia tentang seluruh rakyat Yehuda, pada TAHUN KEEMPAT pemerintahan Yoyakim bin Yosia, raja Yehuda (yaitu tahun pertama pemerintahan Nebukadnezar, raja Babel), (YER 25:1)

event_name: Baruch records the scroll of Jeremiah chapter 31
event_description: In the FOURTH YEAR of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah, king of Judah, this word came to Jeremiah from the LORD, saying, “Take a scroll and write on it all the words which I have spoken to you concerning Israel and concerning Judah, and concerning all the nations, from the day I first spoke to you, from the days of Josiah, even to this day. (JER 36:1-2)
event_year_calculation: The year Baruch recorded Jeremiah's words is the year Eliakim's reign began (3353) + 4 years = 3357
event_notes:
Also, JER 45 and 46.
[Shulman date p173]
[Ussher date p95]
event_description_id: Pada TAHUN KEEMPAT pemerintahan Yoyakim bin Yosia, raja Yehuda, datanglah firman ini kepada Yeremia dari TUHAN, yang berbunyi: “Ambillah sebuah gulungan dan tulislah di atasnya segala perkataan yang telah Kukatakan kepadamu tentang Israel, dan tentang Yehuda, dan tentang segala bangsa, sejak pertama kali Aku berbicara kepadamu, sejak zaman Yosia, sampai sekarang.” (YER 36:1-2)

event_name: Judah's fast during the month of Av (the ninth month)
event_description: Now in the FIFTH YEAR of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah, king of Judah, in the ninth month, all the people in Jerusalem and all the people who came from the cities of Judah to Jerusalem proclaimed a fast before the LORD. (JER 36:9)
event_year_calculation: The year Judah fasts during Jehoiakim's reign is the year Eliakim's reign began (3353) + 5 years = 3358
event_notes: [Ussher date p96]
peristiwa: Puasa Yehuda pada bulan Av (bulan kesembilan)
event_description_id: Pada tahun kelima pemerintahan Yoyakim bin Yosia, raja Yehuda, pada bulan kesembilan, seluruh rakyat Yerusalem dan seluruh rakyat yang datang dari kota-kota Yehuda ke Yerusalem mengumumkan puasa di hadapan TUHAN. (YER 36:9)

event_description:
Now in the second year of the reign of Nebuchadnezzar, Nebuchadnezzar had dreams; and his spirit was troubled and his sleep left him.
 
So the decree went forth that the wise men should be slain; and they looked for Daniel and his friended to kill them. (DAN 2:1, 13)
event_year_calculation: The year Nebuchadnezzar had dreams is the year his reign began (3357) + 2 years = 3359
event_notes:
[Shulman date p173]
[Ussher date p97]
event_description_id:
Pada tahun kedua pemerintahan Nebukadnezar, Nebukadnezar mendapat mimpi; dan semangatnya gelisah dan tidurnya meninggalkan dia.
 
Maka dikeluarkanlah keputusan bahwa orang-orang majus harus dibunuh; dan mereka mencari Daniel dan temannya untuk membunuh mereka. (DAN 2:1, 13)

event_description: In his days Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came up, and Jehoiakim became his servant for three years; then he turned and rebelled against him. (2KI 24:1)
event_year_calculation: The year Eliakim's (Jehoiakim's) vassalage began is the year his reign ended (3364) - the length of his vassalage (3) = 3328
event_notes: [Ussher date p97]
event_description_id: Pada zamannya muncullah Nebukadnezar, raja Babel, dan Yoyakim menjadi pelayannya selama tiga tahun; kemudian dia berbalik dan memberontak melawannya. (2KI 24:1)

event_name: Eliakim's (Jehoiakim's) reign as king over Judah ends
event_description: Jehoiakim was twenty-five years old when he became king, and he reigned ELEVEN YEARS in Jerusalem; and his mother’s name was Zebidah the daughter of Pedaiah of Rumah. (2KI 23:36)
event_year_calculation: The year Eliakim's (Jehoiakim's) reign ended is the year his reign began (3353) + the length of his reign (11) = 3364
event_notes:
[Shulman date p83]
[Ussher date p98]
peristiwa: Pemerintahan Eliakim (Yoyakim) sebagai raja atas Yehuda berakhir
event_description_id: Yoyakim berumur dua puluh lima tahun ketika ia menjadi raja, dan ia memerintah SEBELAS TAHUN di Yerusalem; dan nama ibunya adalah Zebidah putri Pedaya Rumah. (2KI 23:36)

event_description: So Jehoiakim slept with his fathers, and Jehoiachin his son became king in his place. (2KI 24:6)
event_year_calculation: The year of Eliakim_2's death (3364) is the year his reign ended = 3364
event_notes: [Ussher date p98]
event_description_id: Maka Yoyakim tidur bersama nenek moyangnya, dan Yoyakhin putranya menjadi raja menggantikan dia. (2KI 24:6)

event_name: Jehoiachin's reign as king over Judah begins
event_description: So Jehoiakim slept with his fathers, and Jehoiachin his son became king in his place. (2KI 24:6)
event_year_calculation: The year Jehoiachin's (Jehoiakim's) reign began is the year Eliakim's reign ended = 3364
event_notes:
[Shulman date p83]
[Ussher date p98]
peristiwa: Pemerintahan Yoyakhin sebagai raja atas Yehuda dimulai
event_description_id: Maka Yoyakim tidur bersama nenek moyangnya, dan Yoyakhin putranya menjadi raja menggantikan dia. (2KI 24:6)

event_name: Jehoiachin's reign as king over Judah ends*
event_description: Jehoiachin was eighteen years old when he became king, and he reigned THREE MONTHS in Jerusalem; and his mother’s name was Nehushta the daughter of Elnathan of Jerusalem. (2KI 24:8)
event_year_calculation: The year Jehoiachin's reign ended is the year his reign began (3364) + the length of his reign (3 months) = 3365
event_notes:
*HYPOTHESIS: 
The three months began in A.H. 3364 and ended in A.H. 3365.  This ensures the remaining regnal dates end with the destruction of the Temple in 586 BCE.
[Shulman date p83]
[Ussher date p98]
peristiwa: Pemerintahan Yoyakhin sebagai raja atas Yehuda berakhir*
event_description_id: Yoyakhin berusia delapan belas tahun ketika ia menjadi raja, dan ia memerintah TIGA BULAN di Yerusalem; dan nama ibunya adalah Nehushta putri Elnatan dari Yerusalem. (2KI 24:8)

event_name: Jehoiachin's exile and imprisonment in Babylon began
event_description: So he led Jehoiachin away into exile to Babylon; also the king’s mother and the king’s wives and his officials and the leading men of the land, he led away into exile from Jerusalem to Babylon. (2KI 24:15)
event_year_calculation: The year Jehoiachin's exile began is the year his reign ended = 3365
event_notes:
[Shulman date p142]
[Ussher date p98]
peristiwa: Pengasingan dan pemenjaraan Yoyakhin di Babilonia dimulai
event_description_id: Maka dia membawa Yoyakhin pergi ke pengasingan di Babel; juga ibu raja dan istri-istri raja serta para pejabatnya dan para pemimpin negeri itu, dia bawa ke pengasingan dari Yerusalem ke Babel. (2KI 24:15)

event_name: Mattaniah's (Zedekiah's) reign as king over Judah begins
event_description: Then the king of Babylon made his uncle Mattaniah king in his place, and changed his name to Zedekiah. (2KI 24:17)
event_year_calculation: The year Mattaniah's (Zedekiah's) reign began is the year Jehoiachin's reign ended = 3365
event_notes:
[Shulman date p83]
[Ussher date p99]
peristiwa: Pemerintahan Matania (Zedekia) sebagai raja atas Yehuda dimulai
event_description_id: Kemudian raja Babel mengangkat pamannya, Matania, menjadi raja menggantikan dia, dan mengganti namanya menjadi Zedekia. (2KI 24:17)

event_description: Jubilee #16
event_year_calculation: The year of Jubilee #16 is the year of the previous Jubilee (3319) + a Jubilee period of years (50) = 3369
event_notes: [Ussher date p89]
event_description_id: Yobel #16

event_description: (On the fifth of the month in the fifth year of King Jehoiachin’s exile, the word of the LORD came expressly to Ezekiel the priest, son of Buzi, in the land of the Chaldeans by the river Chebar; and there the hand of the LORD came upon him.) (EZE 1:2-3)
event_year_calculation: The year Ezekiel's time as prophet begins is in year 5 of Jehoiachin's exile (3365 + 5) = 3370
event_notes:
[Shulman date p83]
[Ussher date p98]
event_description_id: (Pada tanggal lima bulan tahun kelima masa pembuangan Raja Yoyakhin, datanglah firman TUHAN dengan tegas kepada Imam Yehezkiel bin Buzi, di negeri orang Kasdim di tepi sungai Kebar; dan di sanalah tangan TUHAN turun ke atas dia.) (Yeh 1:2-3)

event_description: Now it came about in the thirtieth year, on the fifth day of the fourth month, while I was by the river Chebar among the exiles, the heavens were opened and I saw visions of God. (On the fifth of the month in the FIFTH YEAR of King Jehoiachin's exile, the word of the LORD came expressly to Ezekiel the priest, son of Buzi, in the land of the Chaldeans by the river Chebar; and there the hand of the LORD came upon him.) (EZE 1:1-3)
event_year_calculation: The year Ezekiel had visions is the year Jehoiachin's exile began (3365) + 5 years = 3370
event_notes:
[Shulman date p83]
[Ussher date p99]
event_description_id: Ketika itu terjadi pada tahun ketiga puluh, pada hari kelima bulan keempat, ketika aku berada di tepi sungai Kebar di antara orang-orang buangan, langit terbuka dan aku melihat penglihatan tentang Allah. (Pada tanggal lima bulan TAHUN KELIMA masa pembuangan Raja Yoyakhin, datanglah firman TUHAN dengan tegas kepada Imam Yehezkiel bin Buzi, di negeri orang Kasdim di tepi sungai Kebar; dan di sanalah tangan TUHAN menyentuh dia.) (Yeh 1:1-3)

event_name: Nebuchadnezzar began his seige of Jerusalem
event_description: Now in the NINTH YEAR of his reign, on the tenth day of the tenth month, Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came, he and all his army, against Jerusalem, camped against it and built a siege wall all around it. (2KI 25:1)
event_year_calculation: The year Nebuchadnezzar's seige of Jerusalem began is nine years after Mattaniah's (Zedekiah's) reign began (3365 + 9) = 3374
event_notes: [Ussher date p102]
event_description_id: Pada tahun kesembilan pemerintahannya, pada hari kesepuluh bulan kesepuluh, datanglah Nebukadnezar, raja Babel, dan seluruh pasukannya menyerang Yerusalem, lalu berkemah di sana dan mendirikan tembok pengepungan di sekelilingnya. (2KI 25:1)

event_name: Mattaniah's (Zedekiah's) reign as king over Judah ends
event_description: Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king, and he reigned ELEVEN YEARS in Jerusalem; and his mother’s name was Hamutal the daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah. (2KI 24:18)
event_year_calculation: The year Mattaniah's (Zedekiah's) reign ended is the year his reign began (3365) + the length of his reign () = 3365
event_notes:
[Shulman date p83]
[Ussher date p104]
peristiwa: Pemerintahan Matania (Zedekia) sebagai raja atas Yehuda berakhir
event_description_id: Zedekia berumur dua puluh satu tahun ketika dia menjadi raja, dan dia memerintah SEBELAS TAHUN di Yerusalem; dan nama ibunya adalah Hamutal putri Yeremia dari Libna. (2KI 24:18)

event_name: Nebuchadnezzar ended his seige of Jerusalem
event_description: So the city was under siege until the ELEVENTH YEAR of King Zedekiah. (2KI 25:2)
event_year_calculation: The year Nebuchadnezzar's seige of Jerusalem ended is eleven years after Mattaniah's (Zedekiah's) reign began (3376 + 11) = 3387
event_notes: [Ussher date p104]
event_description_id: Maka kota itu dikepung sampai TAHUN KESEBELAS pemerintahan Raja Zedekia. (2KI 25:2)

event_name: Destruction of Solomon's Temple (the house of the LORD) by Nebuzaradan
event_description: Now on the seventh day of the fifth month, which was the NINETEENTH YEAR of King Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard, a servant of the king of Babylon, came to Jerusalem.  He burned the house of the LORD, the king’s house, and all the houses of Jerusalem; even every great house he burned with fire. (2KI 25:8-9)
event_year_calculation: The year Nebuzaradan destroyed the Temple (3376) is 19 years after Nebuchadnezzar's reign began (3357 + 19) = 3376
event_notes:

event_name: Cyrus's reign as king over Persia begins
event_description: The start of Cyrus the Great’s reign is primarily associated with two distinct contexts: his rule over Persia (as king of Anshan or Persia) and his conquest of Babylon, which marked his dominance over the Babylonian Empire. The most widely accepted years, based on ancient sources, are 559 BCE for his reign over Persia and 539 BCE for his rule over Babylon.
event_year_calculation: The year Cyrus' reign over Persia began (according to extra-biblical sources) is 559 BCE which is 27 years after the destruction of the Temple in 586 (3376 AH + 27) = 3403
event_notes:
*EXTRA-BIBLICAL SOURCES:
The Nabonidus Chronicle, Babylonian contract tablets, and Ptolemy’s Canon, combined with Herodotus (Histories 1.46–214) and Berossus (via Josephus), indicate Cyrus became king of Anshan (a precursor to his Persian rule) around 559 BCE. This marks the beginning of his reign over the Persian tribes before his conquests of Media, Lydia, and Babylon.
[Ussher date p111]
peristiwa: Pemerintahan Cyrus sebagai raja atas Persia dimulai
event_description_id: Awal pemerintahan Cyrus Agung terutama dikaitkan dengan dua konteks berbeda: pemerintahannya atas Persia (sebagai raja Anshan atau Persia) dan penaklukannya atas Babilonia, yang menandai dominasinya atas Kekaisaran Babilonia. Tahun-tahun yang paling banyak diterima, berdasarkan sumber-sumber kuno, adalah tahun 559 SM untuk pemerintahannya atas Persia dan tahun 539 SM untuk pemerintahannya atas Babilonia.

event_name: Evil-merodach's reign as king over Babylon ends
event_description: Evil-merodach reigned two years
event_year_calculation: The year Evil-merodach's reign ended is the year his reign began (3402) + the length of his reign (2 years) = 3404
event_notes:
*EXTRA-BIBLICAL SOURCES:
- Berossus, a Hellenistic-era Babylonian priest and historian, is cited in later works (e.g., by Josephus and Eusebius) stating that Evil-Merodach reigned for two years before being assassinated by his brother-in-law, Neriglissar. His account, preserved in fragments, describes Evil-Merodach’s rule as "lawless and outrageous," leading to his overthrow after a short reign. This aligns with the two-year timeframe.
- Abydenus, another ancient historian, is referenced in the McClintock and Strong Biblical Cyclopedia as assigning Evil-Merodach a reign of two years. His works, though surviving only in fragments, corroborate Berossus’ account of the brief rule ending with Neriglissar’s usurpation.
- The Canon of Ptolemy, a chronological list of kings, records Evil-Merodach (under the name Ilvoradam or similar) as reigning for two years, from 562 to 560 BCE. This astronomical and historical document is considered a reliable source for Babylonian chronology.
- Approximately 30 cuneiform contract tablets dated to Evil-Merodach’s reign have been discovered, covering a period of about two years and five months (562–560 BCE). These tablets, used for economic transactions, provide direct evidence of his rule’s duration, as they cease after August 560 BCE, when Neriglissar’s documents appear.
- The Uruk King List, a cuneiform document, records the reigns of Babylonian kings. While it does not provide detailed narratives, it confirms Evil-Merodach’s reign as brief, consistent with the two-year period noted in other sources.
[Shulman date p143]
[Ussher date p111]

It notes that Nabonidus entrusted the kingship to Belshazzar in his third year (ca. 553 BCE) while Nabonidus was in Tema (Arabia). This suggests Belshazzar acted as co-regent or ruler in Babylon from around 553 BCE until the fall of Babylon in 539 BCE, implying a period of about 14 years.

event_name: Belshazzar's reign as king over Babylon ends
event_description: Belshazzar's co-regency with Nabonidus over the Babylonian empire ended with his defeat by Cyrus.
event_year_calculation: The year Belshazzar's reign ended is the year Cyrus' reign began = 3423
event_notes:
*EXTRA-BIBLICAL SOURCES:
- The Nabonidus Chronicle, a Babylonian cuneiform text, documents the reign of Nabonidus, Belshazzar’s father, from 556 to 539 BCE. It notes that Nabonidus entrusted the kingship to Belshazzar in his third year (ca. 553 BCE) while Nabonidus was in Tema (Arabia). This suggests Belshazzar acted as co-regent or ruler in Babylon from around 553 BCE until the fall of Babylon in 539 BCE, implying a period of about 14 years. The chronicle does not explicitly state the duration but provides key chronological markers.
- The Verse Account of Nabonidus (Cuneiform Text, 6th Century BCE), a propagandistic cuneiform text, likely composed after the Persian conquest, describes Nabonidus’ absence from Babylon and his delegation of authority to his son (presumably Belshazzar). It aligns with the Nabonidus Chronicle, indicating Belshazzar’s rule began around Nabonidus’ third year (553 BCE) and continued until 539 BCE, supporting a roughly 14-year period of influence.
- Numerous cuneiform tablets from Babylon, dated to the reign of Nabonidus, mention Belshazzar in roles of authority, such as overseeing transactions or military matters. These tablets span from around 553 BCE (Nabonidus’ third year) to 539 BCE, suggesting Belshazzar’s active rule or co-regency during this period. While they do not explicitly state a reign length, the consistent references over these years imply about 14 years of prominence.
- Herodotus, in his Histories (Book 1), does not directly name Belshazzar but refers to a Babylonian king (possibly Belshazzar or Nabonidus) during the fall of Babylon in 539 BCE. He does not provide a reign length, but his account aligns with the timeline of Babylonian rule ending in 539 BCE, consistent with Belshazzar’s final year as co-regent.
- In his Cyropaedia, Xenophon describes the fall of Babylon and mentions a young king ruling at the time, likely Belshazzar. While he does not specify the length of the reign, his narrative supports the timeline of 539 BCE as the end of Belshazzar’s rule, corroborating other sources that place Belshazzar in power during Nabonidus’ later years.
- Berossus, as cited by later authors like Josephus, describes the final years of Babylonian rule and mentions Belshazzar (under the name "Nabonnedus’ son") as ruling when Babylon fell to Cyrus in 539 BCE. While Berossus does not explicitly state the duration of Belshazzar’s rule, his account implies a shorter period of authority under Nabonidus, consistent with the co-regency starting around 553 BCE.
[Shulman date p143]
[Ussher date p116]

event_name: Cyrus's reign as king over Babylon begins
event_description: The start of Cyrus the Great’s reign is primarily associated with two distinct contexts: his rule over Persia (as king of Anshan or Persia) and his conquest of Babylon, which marked his dominance over the Babylonian Empire. The most widely accepted years, based on ancient sources, are 559 BCE for his reign over Persia and 539 BCE for his rule over Babylon.
event_year_calculation: The year Cyrus_1's reign began (according to extra-biblical sources) was in 539 BCE, 47 years after the destruction of the Temple in Jerusalem in 586 (3376 AH + 47) = 3423
event_notes:
*EXTRA-BIBLICAL SOURCES:
- The Nabonidus Chronicle, a Babylonian cuneiform document, explicitly records the fall of Babylon to Cyrus in the 17th year of Nabonidus’ reign, which corresponds to 539 BCE. It describes Cyrus’ army entering Babylon without a battle in the month of Tishri (October) 539 BCE, after defeating Babylonian forces at Opis.  This is the most precise and authoritative source for dating Cyrus’ conquest to 539 BCE.
- The Cyrus Cylinder, a propagandistic cuneiform document, details Cyrus’ capture of Babylon and his claim to legitimate rule. While it does not provide an exact year, it describes his peaceful entry into Babylon and restoration of temples, aligning with the Nabonidus Chronicle’s account of 539 BCE.
- Cuneiform economic and administrative tablets from Babylon show a transition in dating formulas from Nabonidus’ reign to Cyrus’ reign in 539 BCE. Tablets dated to Nabonidus cease in October 539 BCE, and those dated to “Year 1 of Cyrus, King of Babylon” begin shortly after, confirming the conquest and start of his reign in 539 BCE. These records provide precise chronological evidence.
- The Canon of Ptolemy, a chronological list of kings used for astronomical calculations, records Cyrus as ruler of Babylon starting in 538/539 BCE (following Nabonidus’ 17-year reign). While compiled later, it relies on earlier Babylonian records and aligns with the Nabonidus Chronicle, placing Cyrus’ accession over Babylon in 539 BCE.
- In his Histories (Book 1.178–191), Herodotus describes Cyrus’ conquest of Babylon, including diverting the Euphrates River to enter the city. While he does not provide a specific year, his narrative aligns with the Babylonian timeline of 539 BCE, as it follows Cyrus’ earlier conquests (e.g., Lydia in 546 BCE) and precedes later Persian campaigns. His account supports the general chronology established by Babylonian sources.
- Xenophon’s Cyropaedia (Book 7) recounts Cyrus’ capture of Babylon, describing a nighttime assault during a festival. Like Herodotus, Xenophon does not specify a year, but his narrative is consistent with the 539 BCE conquest described in Babylonian records, reinforcing the timeline.
- Berossus, as cited by later authors like Josephus (Against Apion 1.20), describes Cyrus’ conquest of Babylon after defeating Nabonidus. He does not explicitly state 539 BCE, but his account of Cyrus’ victory and rule over Babylon aligns with the Nabonidus Chronicle’s timeline, supporting the start of Cyrus’ reign in 539 BCE.
[Shulman date p143]
[Ussher date p116]

event_notes:

Herodotus (Histories 1.201–214) provides the primary account, describing Cyrus’ death in a campaign against the Massagetae, dated to around 530 BCE based on his 29-year reign (559–530 BCE). event_notes:
*EXTRA-BIBLICAL SOURCES:
- The Behistun Inscription, a monumental rock relief and trilingual cuneiform inscription, commissioned by Darius I, details his rise to power. It describes how Darius, a Persian noble, overthrew Gaumata (a Magian usurper claiming to be Bardiya, son of Cyrus) in 522 BCE, thus becoming king.
- The Babylonian Contract Tablets, economic and administrative tablets from Babylon record transactions dated by regnal years of Persian kings. Tablets dated to “Year 1 of Darius” begin in late 522 BCE, after those dated to Bardiya (March–September 522 BCE).
- The Canon of Ptolemy, a chronological list of kings used for astronomical calculations, lists Darius I as succeeding Cambyses II and Bardiya, with his reign beginning in 522/521 BCE.
- In his Histories (Book 3.61–88), Herodotus narrates Darius’ rise to power, describing how he and six Persian nobles conspired to overthrow the usurper “Smerdis” (Gaumata, posing as Bardiya) in 522 BCE.
- In his Persica, Ctesias offers an alternative account of Persian history, preserved in fragments by later authors like Photius. He mentions Darius’ rise after Cambyses but includes less reliable details.
[Shulman date p143]
[Ussher date p125]

event_description: In the FIRST YEAR of Darius the son of Ahasuerus, of Median descent, who was made king over the kingdom of the Chaldeans-- in the first year of his reign, I, Daniel, observed in the books the number of the years which was revealed as the word of the LORD to Jeremiah the prophet for the completion of the desolations of Jerusalem, namely, seventy years. (DAN 9:1-2)
event_year_calculation: The year of Gabriel's message to Daniel is the first year of Darius' reign = 3440
event_notes: This was Darius WHOSE SON WAS Ahasuerus (rather than the "son of" Ahasuerus), i.e., Darius I rather than Darius II.  For this to be Darius II, Daniel would be over 200 years old.

event_notes:

[Ussher date p160]  Malachi, the last of the prophets, was contemporary with Nehemiah. This we gather from the following. He nowhere exhorts the people to build the temple as Haggai and Zechariah did.  Since the Temple was now built, he reproved those disorders among the Jews which Nehemiah at his second return with a new commission did also. These are, the marriage with foreign women, Mal 2:11 withholding of tithes, Mal 3:8 and abuses in the worship of God. Mal 1:13 2:8.

event_name: Darius's reign as king over Persia ends
event_description: Darius died in October 486 BCE, likely of natural causes, after planning a second invasion of Greece. His son, Xerxes I, succeeded him.
event_year_calculation: The year Darius's reign ended is the his reign began (3440) + the length of his reign (36) = 3476
event_notes:
*EXTRA-BIBLICAL SOURCES:
- The Behistun Inscription, a monumental rock relief and trilingual cuneiform inscription, commissioned by Darius I, details his rise to power. It describes how Darius, a Persian noble, overthrew Gaumata (a Magian usurper claiming to be Bardiya, son of Cyrus) in 522 BCE, thus becoming king.
- The Babylonian Contract Tablets, economic and administrative tablets from Babylon record transactions dated by regnal years of Persian kings. Tablets dated to “Year 1 of Darius” begin in late 522 BCE, after those dated to Bardiya (March–September 522 BCE).
- The Canon of Ptolemy, a chronological list of kings used for astronomical calculations, lists Darius I as succeeding Cambyses II and Bardiya, with his reign beginning in 522/521 BCE.
- In his Histories (Book 3.61–88), Herodotus narrates Darius’ rise to power, describing how he and six Persian nobles conspired to overthrow the usurper “Smerdis” (Gaumata, posing as Bardiya) in 522 BCE.
- In his Persica, Ctesias offers an alternative account of Persian history, preserved in fragments by later authors like Photius. He mentions Darius’ rise after Cambyses but includes less reliable details.
peristiwa: Pemerintahan Darius sebagai raja atas Persia berakhir
event_description_id: Darius meninggal pada bulan Oktober 486 SM, kemungkinan besar karena sebab alamiah, setelah merencanakan invasi kedua ke Yunani. Putranya, Xerxes I, menggantikannya.

event_name: Ahasuerus's (Xerxes') reign as king over Persia begins
event_description: The son of Darius, Xerxes I, succeeded him.
event_year_calculation: The year Ahasuerus's reign began is the year Darius' reign ended = 3476
event_notes:
Most scholars identify Ahasuerus in Esther with Xerxes I (486–465 BCE). The description of his vast empire (127 provinces in EST 1:1) and opulent court aligns with Herodotus’ portrayal of Xerxes’ wealth and campaigns (Histories 7–9). The Greek form of Xerxes (Xerxes) matches the Hebrew Achashverosh via the Old Persian Xšayārša.  The account of the book of Esther, with its Persian court intrigue and reference to Susa (Shushan), fits Xerxes I’s reign.  No ancient sources explicitly name Xerxes as Ahasuerus, but the linguistic and historical fit is strong. The absence of precise dates in Esther allows flexibility, but Xerxes I is the consensus.  Xerxes I preceded Artaxerxes I, his son. Thus, Ahasuerus (Xerxes I) in Esther is not the same person as Artaxerxes in Ezra or Nehemiah.

EXTRA-BIBLICAL SOURCES:
Babylonian contract tablets dated to Darius I’s reign (522–486 BCE) cease in late 486 BCE (around October/November), and tablets dated to “Year 1 of Xerxes, King of Persia and Media” begin shortly thereafter, marking his accession in late 486 BCE. The transition is seamless, indicating Xerxes’ immediate recognition as king.
[Shulman date p143]
[Ussher date p119]
peristiwa: Pemerintahan Ahasuerus (Xerxes) sebagai raja atas Persia dimulai

event_description: Now it took place in the days of Ahasuerus, the Ahasuerus who reigned from India to Ethiopia over 127 provinces, in those days as King Ahasuerus sat on his royal throne which was at the citadel in Susa, in the THIRD YEAR of his reign he gave a banquet for all his princes and attendants, the army officers of Persia and Media, the nobles and the princes of his provinces being in his presence. (EST 1:1-3)
event_year_calculation: The year Vashti was banished was the year Ahasuerus' reign began (3476) + 3 years = 3479
event_notes:
Xerxes’ preparations for the Second Persian Invasion of Greece (480–479 BCE) dominate his early reign:
- 484–483 BCE: Planning and mobilization, including the council in 483 BCE (Herodotus 7.8).
- 480 BCE: Invasion, with battles at Thermopylae and Salamis (Herodotus 7–8).
- 479 BCE: Defeat at Plataea and Mycale, followed by Xerxes’ return to Persia (Herodotus 9).

The banquet in Esther 1 (483 BCE) likely served to consolidate support for the invasion, and Vashti’s banishment occurred during this period of heightened court activity.
[Shulman date p149]
[Ussher date p127]
event_description_id: Sekarang hal itu terjadi pada masa Ahasuerus, sang Ahasuerus yang memerintah dari India hingga Etiopia atas 127 propinsi, pada masa itu ketika Raja Ahasuerus duduk di singgasana kerajaannya yang berada di benteng di Susa, pada TAHUN KETIGA pemerintahannya ia mengadakan jamuan makan untuk semua pangeran dan pengiringnya, para perwira tentara Persia dan Media, para bangsawan dan para pangeran dari provinsi-provinsinya yang berada di hadapannya. (EST 1:1-3)

event_notes:

event_name: Ahasuerus's (Xerxes') reign as king over Persia ends
event_description: Xerxes I reigned for twenty-one years.
event_year_calculation: The year Ahasuerus's reign ended is the year his reign began (3476)  + the length of his reign (21) years = 3497
event_notes:
*EXTRA-BIBLICAL SOURCES:
- Babylonian contract tablets dated to “Year 21 of Xerxes” appear until mid-465 BCE (around August), after which tablets dated to “Year 1 of Artaxerxes” begin, indicating Xerxes’ death in the summer of 465 BCE. Some tablets note a brief period of instability, possibly due to the assassination.
- Ptolemy’s Canon lists Xerxes I’s reign as 21 years, ending in 465 BCE, with Artaxerxes I succeeding him.
- Herodotus’ Histories does not explicitly describe Xerxes’ death, as his account focuses on the Greek wars (480–479 BCE) and ended before 465 BCE.
- In his Persica, preserved in fragments by later authors like Photius, Ctesias describes Xerxes’ assassination by a courtier, Artabanus, in a palace conspiracy.
- In his Library of History (Book 11.69), Diodorus provides a later account of Xerxes’ assassination, attributing it to Artabanus, who briefly usurped power before Artaxerxes I’s rise.
- The Persepolis Fortification and Treasury Tablets are administrative records continue under Xerxes until his death until mid-465 BCE, after which Artaxerxes I’s tablets begin.  This transition supports Xerxes’ death in 465 BCE.
[Shulman date p143]

event_notes:
*EXTRA-BIBLICAL SOURCES:
- Babylonian contract tablets dated to “Year 21 of Xerxes” appear until mid-465 BCE (around August), after which tablets dated to “Year 1 of Artaxerxes” begin, indicating Xerxes’ death in the summer of 465 BCE. Some tablets note a brief period of instability, possibly due to the assassination.
- Ptolemy’s Canon lists Xerxes I’s reign as 21 years, ending in 465 BCE, with Artaxerxes I succeeding him.
- In his Library of History (Book 11.69), Diodorus provides a later account of Xerxes’ assassination, attributing it to Artabanus, who briefly usurped power before Artaxerxes I’s rise.
- The Persepolis Fortification and Treasury Tablets are administrative records continue under Xerxes until his death until mid-465 BCE, after which Artaxerxes I’s tablets begin.  This transition supports Xerxes’ death in 465 BCE.
[Shulman date p149]
peristiwa: Pemerintahan Artaxerxes sebagai raja atas Persia dimulai
event_description_id: Putra Xerxes I, Artaxerxes, menggantikannya.

event_name: Artaxerxes's reign as king over Persia ends
event_description: Artaxerxes I reigned 41 years.
event_year_calculation: The year Artaxerxes's reign ended is the year his reign began (3497) + the length of his reign (41) = 3538
event_notes:
*EXTRA-BIBLICAL SOURCES:
- Babylonian contract tablets dated to “Year 41 of Artaxerxes” appear until late 424 BCE (around December), after which a brief period of tablets dated to “Year 1 of Xerxes II” (Artaxerxes I’s son) and “Sogdianus” (a rival) emerges, followed by “Year 1 of Darius II” in early 423 BCE. This indicates Artaxerxes I’s death in late 424 BCE, with a short succession crisis.
- Ptolemy’s Canon lists Artaxerxes I’s reign as 41 years, ending in 424 BCE, with Darius II succeeding him.
- In his Persica, Ctesias describes Artaxerxes I’s death and the subsequent succession struggle involving Xerxes II, Sogdianus, and Darius II (Ochus).
- In his Library of History (Book 12.71), Diodorus recounts Artaxerxes I’s death and the brief reigns of Xerxes II and Sogdianus before Darius II’s accession.
- The Elephantine Papyri continue to use Artaxerxes I’s regnal years until his death.
- 
Persepolis Treasury Tablets dated to Artaxerxes I’s reign continue until late 424 BCE, with a transition to Darius II’s records in 423 BCE, supporting Artaxerxes’ death in 424 BCE.
peristiwa: Pemerintahan Artaxerxes sebagai raja atas Persia berakhir
event_description_id: Artaxerxes I memerintah selama 41 tahun.
